###############################################################################
# Adaptive Rejection Sampling (ARS)
# Based on Gilks, W.R. & Wild, P. (1992)
# "Adaptive Rejection Sampling for Gibbs Sampling"
# Journal of the Royal Statistical Society: Series C, 41(2), 337-348.
###############################################################################

# --- Auxiliary / Module Functions ---

#' Evaluate the log of a density function safely
#' @param f density function (possibly unnormalized)
#' @param x point(s) at which to evaluate
#' @return log(f(x)), with protection against f(x) <= 0
eval_log_density <- function(f, x) {
  fx <- f(x)
  if (any(is.na(fx) | is.nan(fx))) {
    stop("Density function returned NA/NaN.")
  }
  if (any(fx < 0)) {
    stop("Density function returned negative value.")
  }
  result <- rep(-Inf, length(fx))
  pos <- fx > 0
  result[pos] <- log(fx[pos])
  return(result)
}

#' Check log-concavity at three consecutive abscissae
#' If h(x) = log(f(x)), then log-concavity requires:
#'   (h(x2) - h(x1))/(x2 - x1) >= (h(x3) - h(x2))/(x3 - x2)
#' for all x1 < x2 < x3
#' @param abscissae sorted vector of x values
#' @param h_vals corresponding log-density values h(x)
#' @return TRUE if log-concave, else stops with error
check_log_concavity <- function(abscissae, h_vals) {
  n <- length(abscissae)
  if (n < 3) return(TRUE)
  for (i in 1:(n - 2)) {
    x1 <- abscissae[i];   h1 <- h_vals[i]
    x2 <- abscissae[i+1]; h2 <- h_vals[i+1]
    x3 <- abscissae[i+2]; h3 <- h_vals[i+2]
    slope_left  <- (h2 - h1) / (x2 - x1)
    slope_right <- (h3 - h2) / (x3 - x2)
    if (slope_right > slope_left + 1e-10) {
      stop(paste0(
        "Log-concavity violated at x = ", x2,
        ". Left slope = ", round(slope_left, 6),
        ", Right slope = ", round(slope_right, 6),
        ". The density may not be log-concave."
      ))
    }
  }
  return(TRUE)
}

#' Compute the slopes between consecutive abscissae on the log-density
#' @param abscissae sorted x values
#' @param h_vals corresponding h(x)
#' @return vector of slopes of length (n-1)
compute_slopes <- function(abscissae, h_vals) {
  n <- length(abscissae)
  slopes <- (h_vals[2:n] - h_vals[1:(n-1)]) / (abscissae[2:n] - abscissae[1:(n-1)])
  return(slopes)
}

#' Compute the upper hull (piecewise-linear envelope)
#' The upper hull at each abscissa j uses the tangent lines from adjacent segments.
#' For j=1: use slope from segment (x1, x2)
#' For j=k (last): use slope from segment (x_{k-1}, x_k)
#' For interior j: the tangent from left segment and right segment
#'
#' Returns the intersection points z between consecutive tangent lines,
#' plus the slopes and intercepts of each tangent segment.
#'
#' @param abscissae sorted x values
#' @param h_vals corresponding h(x)
#' @param lb lower bound of domain
#' @param ub upper bound of domain
#' @return list with z (intersection points), slopes, intercepts for upper hull
compute_upper_hull <- function(abscissae, h_vals, lb, ub) {
  k <- length(abscissae)

  # Slopes of secant lines between consecutive abscissae
  secant_slopes <- compute_slopes(abscissae, h_vals)

  # Each tangent line j: u_j(x) = h_j + s_j * (x - x_j)
  # where s_j is the slope assigned to abscissa j
  # For the upper hull in ARS:
  #   - segment j uses the line through (x_j, h_j) and (x_{j+1}, h_{j+1})
  #   i.e., slope = secant_slope[j], intercept at x_j
  # There are k-1 segments. The hull extends to lb on the left and ub on the right.

  # The upper hull consists of k-1 line segments connecting consecutive points.
  # But in ARS, the upper hull is defined by tangent lines at each abscissa.
  # For log-concave densities, the tangent at x_j has slope between
  # the left secant and right secant.

  # Actually, in Gilks & Wild (1992), the upper hull u_k(x) is the
  # minimum of the tangent lines. With only function values (no derivatives),
  # they use the secant lines. Specifically:
  #
  # The upper hull is piecewise linear with pieces defined by:
  #   piece j (for j = 1, ..., k-1): line through (x_j, h_j) and (x_{j+1}, h_{j+1})
  #
  # The intersection z_j of piece j and piece j+1 gives the breakpoints.

  # For j = 1,...,k-1, line j has:
  #   slope_j = (h_{j+1} - h_j) / (x_{j+1} - x_j)
  #   u_j(x) = h_j + slope_j * (x - x_j)
  #
  # Intersection of line j and line j+1:
  #   h_j + slope_j * (z - x_j) = h_{j+1} + slope_{j+1} * (z - x_{j+1})
  #   z = (h_{j+1} - h_j + slope_j * x_j - slope_{j+1} * x_{j+1}) / (slope_j - slope_{j+1})

  if (k < 2) stop("Need at least 2 abscissae for upper hull.")

  # k-1 segments
  seg_slopes <- secant_slopes
  seg_intercepts <- h_vals[1:(k-1)] - seg_slopes * abscissae[1:(k-1)]
  # u_j(x) = seg_intercepts[j] + seg_slopes[j] * x

  # Intersection points z between consecutive segments
  # z[j] is where segment j meets segment j+1 (for j = 1,...,k-2)
  z_inner <- numeric(0)
  if (k > 2) {
    z_inner <- (seg_intercepts[2:(k-1)] - seg_intercepts[1:(k-2)]) /
               (seg_slopes[1:(k-2)] - seg_slopes[2:(k-1)])
  }

  # Full z vector including domain bounds
  z <- c(lb, z_inner, ub)

  return(list(
    z = z,
    seg_slopes = seg_slopes,
    seg_intercepts = seg_intercepts
  ))
}

#' Evaluate the upper hull at a point x
#' @param x point to evaluate
#' @param hull list from compute_upper_hull
#' @return u(x), the upper hull value
eval_upper_hull <- function(x, hull) {
  z <- hull$z
  seg_slopes <- hull$seg_slopes
  seg_intercepts <- hull$seg_intercepts
  n_seg <- length(seg_slopes)

  # Find which segment x falls in
  # Segment j covers [z[j], z[j+1]]
  seg_idx <- findInterval(x, z, rightmost.closed = TRUE)
  seg_idx <- pmax(1, pmin(seg_idx, n_seg))

  return(seg_intercepts[seg_idx] + seg_slopes[seg_idx] * x)
}

#' Evaluate the lower hull (piecewise linear through abscissae) at a point x
#' @param x point to evaluate
#' @param abscissae sorted x values
#' @param h_vals corresponding h(x) values
#' @return l(x), the lower hull value. -Inf if outside the range of abscissae.
eval_lower_hull <- function(x, abscissae, h_vals) {
  k <- length(abscissae)
  if (x <= abscissae[1] || x >= abscissae[k]) {
    return(-Inf)
  }
  # Find which interval x falls in
  j <- findInterval(x, abscissae)
  j <- pmax(1, pmin(j, k - 1))

  # Linear interpolation
  t <- (x - abscissae[j]) / (abscissae[j+1] - abscissae[j])
  return(h_vals[j] * (1 - t) + h_vals[j+1] * t)
}

#' Sample from the upper hull (piecewise-exponential distribution)
#' The upper hull is a piecewise linear function on log scale, which corresponds
#' to a piecewise exponential distribution.
#' @param n number of samples to draw
#' @param hull list from compute_upper_hull
#' @return vector of n samples from the upper hull distribution
sample_upper_hull <- function(n, hull) {
  z <- hull$z
  seg_slopes <- hull$seg_slopes
  seg_intercepts <- hull$seg_intercepts
  n_seg <- length(seg_slopes)

  # For each segment j on [z_j, z_{j+1}]:
  # The unnormalized density is exp(a_j + b_j * x) where a_j = seg_intercepts[j], b_j = seg_slopes[j]
  # The integral is:
  #   if b_j != 0: exp(a_j) * (exp(b_j * z_{j+1}) - exp(b_j * z_j)) / b_j
  #   if b_j == 0: exp(a_j) * (z_{j+1} - z_j)

  # Compute area under each segment
  areas <- numeric(n_seg)
  for (j in 1:n_seg) {
    lo <- z[j]
    hi <- z[j + 1]
    if (!is.finite(lo)) lo <- z[j + 1] - 10  # fallback for -Inf
    if (!is.finite(hi)) hi <- z[j] + 10       # fallback for +Inf
    a <- seg_intercepts[j]
    b <- seg_slopes[j]

    if (abs(b) < 1e-15) {
      areas[j] <- exp(a) * (hi - lo)
    } else {
      # Integral of exp(a + b*x) from lo to hi
      # = exp(a)/b * (exp(b*hi) - exp(b*lo))
      # Use log-sum-exp for numerical stability
      log_hi <- a + b * hi
      log_lo <- a + b * lo
      if (b > 0) {
        areas[j] <- exp(log_hi) * (1 - exp(log_lo - log_hi)) / b
      } else {
        areas[j] <- exp(log_lo) * (1 - exp(log_hi - log_lo)) / (-b)
      }
    }
    if (is.na(areas[j]) || !is.finite(areas[j]) || areas[j] < 0) {
      areas[j] <- 0
    }
  }

  total_area <- sum(areas)
  if (total_area <= 0 || !is.finite(total_area)) {
    stop("Upper hull has zero or infinite area. Check domain bounds.")
  }
  probs <- areas / total_area

  # Sample segments according to their areas
  seg_choices <- sample.int(n_seg, size = n, replace = TRUE, prob = probs)

  # For each chosen segment, sample from the truncated exponential
  samples <- numeric(n)
  for (i in 1:n) {
    j <- seg_choices[i]
    lo <- z[j]
    hi <- z[j + 1]
    if (!is.finite(lo)) lo <- z[j + 1] - 10
    if (!is.finite(hi)) hi <- z[j] + 10
    b <- seg_slopes[j]

    if (abs(b) < 1e-15) {
      # Uniform on [lo, hi]
      samples[i] <- runif(1, lo, hi)
    } else {
      # Inverse CDF of truncated exponential
      # CDF: F(x) = (exp(b*x) - exp(b*lo)) / (exp(b*hi) - exp(b*lo))
      u <- runif(1)
      # x = log(exp(b*lo) + u * (exp(b*hi) - exp(b*lo))) / b
      # Numerically stable version:
      log_lo <- b * lo
      log_hi <- b * hi
      if (b > 0) {
        samples[i] <- (log_lo + log(1 + u * (exp(log_hi - log_lo) - 1))) / b
      } else {
        samples[i] <- (log_hi + log(1 + (1 - u) * (exp(log_lo - log_hi) - 1))) / b
      }
    }
  }
  return(samples)
}

#' Choose initial abscissae for the ARS algorithm
#' @param lb lower bound
#' @param ub upper bound
#' @param f density function
#' @param n_init number of initial points (default 5)
#' @return vector of initial abscissae
choose_initial_abscissae <- function(lb, ub, f, n_init = 5) {
  # Handle infinite bounds
  if (is.finite(lb) && is.finite(ub)) {
    # Both finite: space evenly
    pts <- seq(lb + (ub - lb) * 0.05, ub - (ub - lb) * 0.05, length.out = n_init)
  } else if (is.finite(lb) && !is.finite(ub)) {
    pts <- lb + (1:n_init) * 1.0
  } else if (!is.finite(lb) && is.finite(ub)) {
    pts <- ub - rev((1:n_init) * 1.0)
  } else {
    # Both infinite: center around 0
    pts <- seq(-2, 2, length.out = n_init)
  }

  # Verify all points have positive density
  vals <- f(pts)
  if (any(is.na(vals) | vals <= 0)) {
    # Try to find good points by searching
    good <- which(vals > 0 & !is.na(vals))
    if (length(good) < 2) {
      stop("Cannot find initial abscissae with positive density. Check domain bounds.")
    }
    pts <- pts[good]
  }

  return(pts)
}

#' Validate inputs to the ars function
#' @param f density function
#' @param n number of samples
#' @param lb lower bound
#' @param ub upper bound
validate_inputs <- function(f, n, lb, ub) {
  if (!is.function(f)) {
    stop("'f' must be a function.")
  }
  if (!is.numeric(n) || length(n) != 1 || n < 1 || n != as.integer(n)) {
    stop("'n' must be a positive integer.")
  }
  if (!is.numeric(lb) || length(lb) != 1) {
    stop("'lb' must be a single numeric value.")
  }
  if (!is.numeric(ub) || length(ub) != 1) {
    stop("'ub' must be a single numeric value.")
  }
  if (lb >= ub) {
    stop("Lower bound must be strictly less than upper bound.")
  }
}

# --- Main ARS Function ---

#' Adaptive Rejection Sampling
#'
#' @param f density function (possibly unnormalized), must be log-concave.
#'          Should accept a vector and return a vector of density values.
#' @param support optional 2-element vector c(lb, ub) for the domain
#' @param n number of samples to generate (default 100)
#' @param lb lower bound of the domain (default -Inf)
#' @param ub upper bound of the domain (default Inf)
#' @param ... additional arguments (currently unused)
#' @return vector of n samples from the target distribution
ars <- function(f, support = NULL, n = 100, lb = -Inf, ub = Inf, ...) {
  # Handle the support vector argument (alternative call pattern)
  if (!is.null(support) && is.numeric(support) && length(support) == 2) {
    lb <- support[1]
    ub <- support[2]
  }

  # Validate inputs
  validate_inputs(f, n, lb, ub)

  # Choose initial abscissae
  abscissae <- choose_initial_abscissae(lb, ub, f)
  h_vals <- eval_log_density(f, abscissae)

  # Check initial log-concavity
  check_log_concavity(abscissae, h_vals)

  # Main sampling loop
  samples <- numeric(n)
  count <- 0
  max_iter <- n * 100  # safety limit to avoid infinite loops
  iter <- 0

  while (count < n && iter < max_iter) {
    iter <- iter + 1

    # Build upper hull
    hull <- compute_upper_hull(abscissae, h_vals, lb, ub)

    # Sample from upper hull
    x_star <- sample_upper_hull(1, hull)

    # Squeeze test
    u <- runif(1)
    u_val <- eval_upper_hull(x_star, hull)
    l_val <- eval_lower_hull(x_star, abscissae, h_vals)

    if (u <= exp(l_val - u_val)) {
      # Accept by squeeze test (no density evaluation needed)
      count <- count + 1
      samples[count] <- x_star
    } else {
      # Need to evaluate the true log-density
      h_star <- eval_log_density(f, x_star)

      if (u <= exp(h_star - u_val)) {
        # Accept by rejection test
        count <- count + 1
        samples[count] <- x_star
      }

      # Update the abscissae with the new point
      insert_pos <- findInterval(x_star, abscissae) + 1
      abscissae <- append(abscissae, x_star, after = insert_pos - 1)
      h_vals <- append(h_vals, h_star, after = insert_pos - 1)

      # Re-check log-concavity with new point
      check_log_concavity(abscissae, h_vals)
    }
  }

  if (count < n) {
    warning(paste("Only generated", count, "out of", n, "requested samples."))
  }

  return(samples)
}

# --- Test Function ---

#' Run formal tests of the ARS implementation
#' Compares samples against known distributions using statistical tests
test <- function() {
  cat("============================================================\n")
  cat("     Adaptive Rejection Sampling (ARS) - Test Suite\n")
  cat("============================================================\n\n")

  pass_count <- 0
  fail_count <- 0

  run_test <- function(name, expr) {
    result <- tryCatch(expr, error = function(e) {
      cat(paste0("  Error: ", e$message, "\n"))
      return(FALSE)
    })
    if (isTRUE(result)) {
      cat(paste0(name, ": PASS\n\n"))
      pass_count <<- pass_count + 1
    } else {
      cat(paste0(name, ": FAIL\n\n"))
      fail_count <<- fail_count + 1
    }
  }

  # ---- Input Validation Tests ----
  cat("--- Input Validation Tests ---\n\n")

  run_test("test_negative_n", {
    cat("  Testing that negative n is rejected...\n")
    err <- tryCatch({ ars(dnorm, n = -5); FALSE },
                    error = function(e) grepl("positive integer", e$message))
    err
  })

  run_test("test_zero_n", {
    cat("  Testing that n=0 is rejected...\n")
    err <- tryCatch({ ars(dnorm, n = 0); FALSE },
                    error = function(e) grepl("positive integer", e$message))
    err
  })

  run_test("test_non_integer_n", {
    cat("  Testing that non-integer n is rejected...\n")
    err <- tryCatch({ ars(dnorm, n = 3.5); FALSE },
                    error = function(e) grepl("positive integer", e$message))
    err
  })

  run_test("test_invalid_bounds", {
    cat("  Testing that lb >= ub is rejected...\n")
    err <- tryCatch({ ars(dnorm, n = 10, lb = 5, ub = 2); FALSE },
                    error = function(e) grepl("strictly less", e$message))
    err
  })

  run_test("test_non_function_f", {
    cat("  Testing that non-function f is rejected...\n")
    err <- tryCatch({ ars("not_a_function", n = 10); FALSE },
                    error = function(e) grepl("must be a function", e$message))
    err
  })

  # ---- Log-Concavity Tests ----
  cat("--- Log-Concavity Tests ---\n\n")

  run_test("test_bimodal_rejection", {
    cat("  Testing rejection of bimodal (non-log-concave) density...\n")
    bimodal <- function(x) 0.5 * dnorm(x, -3, 0.5) + 0.5 * dnorm(x, 3, 0.5)
    err <- tryCatch({ ars(bimodal, n = 1000, lb = -10, ub = 10); FALSE },
                    error = function(e) grepl("[Ll]og-concav", e$message))
    err
  })

  run_test("test_cauchy_rejection", {
    cat("  Testing rejection of Cauchy (non-log-concave on wide domain)...\n")
    # Cauchy is NOT log-concave: log(1/(1+x^2)) = -log(1+x^2) has
    # second derivative (x^2 - 1)/(1+x^2)^2, which is positive for |x|>1
    # However, Cauchy IS log-concave. Let me use t-distribution with df=1.
    # Actually, Cauchy = t(df=1). log f(x) = -log(1+x^2) (up to constant)
    # d^2/dx^2 [-log(1+x^2)] = (2x^2-2)/(1+x^2)^2 which changes sign.
    # So Cauchy IS log-concave only near the center.
    # On a wide enough domain with enough initial points, we should catch it.
    # Actually, the second derivative of -log(1+x^2) is (2(x^2-1))/(1+x^2)^2
    # which is negative for |x| < 1 and positive for |x| > 1.
    # So Cauchy is NOT log-concave. Good.
    err <- tryCatch({
      ars(dcauchy, n = 1000, lb = -100, ub = 100)
      FALSE
    }, error = function(e) grepl("[Ll]og-concav", e$message))
    err
  })

  # ---- Sampling Tests (Standard Normal) ----
  cat("--- Standard Normal Distribution Tests ---\n\n")

  run_test("test_normal_sampling", {
    cat("  Sampling 5000 points from N(0,1)...\n")
    set.seed(42)
    samples <- ars(n = 5000, f = dnorm, lb = -Inf, ub = Inf)
    cat(paste0("  Sample mean: ", round(mean(samples), 4), " (expected ~0)\n"))
    cat(paste0("  Sample sd:   ", round(sd(samples), 4), " (expected ~1)\n"))

    # Save samples
    writeLines(as.character(samples), "/app/normal_samples.txt")
    cat("  Saved samples to /app/normal_samples.txt\n")

    # KS test against N(0,1)
    ks <- ks.test(samples, "pnorm")
    cat(paste0("  KS test p-value: ", round(ks$p.value, 4), "\n"))

    # Check: mean close to 0, sd close to 1, KS p-value > 0.01
    abs(mean(samples)) < 0.15 && abs(sd(samples) - 1) < 0.15 && ks$p.value > 0.01
  })

  run_test("test_normal_bounded", {
    cat("  Sampling 2000 points from N(0,1) on [-5, 5]...\n")
    set.seed(123)
    samples <- ars(dnorm, c(-5, 5), n = 2000)
    cat(paste0("  Sample mean: ", round(mean(samples), 4), "\n"))
    cat(paste0("  Sample sd:   ", round(sd(samples), 4), "\n"))
    cat(paste0("  Min: ", round(min(samples), 4), ", Max: ", round(max(samples), 4), "\n"))

    # All samples should be within bounds
    all(samples >= -5 & samples <= 5) && abs(mean(samples)) < 0.2
  })

  # ---- Sampling Tests (Exponential) ----
  cat("--- Exponential Distribution Tests ---\n\n")

  run_test("test_exponential_sampling", {
    cat("  Sampling 5000 points from Exp(1) on [0, 100]...\n")
    set.seed(99)
    samples <- ars(dexp, c(0.001, 100), n = 5000)
    cat(paste0("  Sample mean: ", round(mean(samples), 4), " (expected ~1)\n"))
    cat(paste0("  Sample sd:   ", round(sd(samples), 4), " (expected ~1)\n"))

    # Save samples
    writeLines(as.character(samples), "/app/exponential_samples.txt")
    cat("  Saved samples to /app/exponential_samples.txt\n")

    # KS test
    ks <- ks.test(samples, "pexp")
    cat(paste0("  KS test p-value: ", round(ks$p.value, 4), "\n"))

    abs(mean(samples) - 1) < 0.15 && ks$p.value > 0.01
  })

  # ---- Sampling Tests (Gamma) ----
  cat("--- Gamma Distribution Tests ---\n\n")

  run_test("test_gamma_sampling", {
    cat("  Sampling 3000 points from Gamma(3, 1) on [0.01, 30]...\n")
    set.seed(77)
    gamma_dens <- function(x) dgamma(x, shape = 3, rate = 1)
    samples <- ars(gamma_dens, c(0.01, 30), n = 3000)
    cat(paste0("  Sample mean: ", round(mean(samples), 4), " (expected ~3)\n"))
    cat(paste0("  Sample sd:   ", round(sd(samples), 4), " (expected ~1.73)\n"))

    ks <- ks.test(samples, "pgamma", shape = 3, rate = 1)
    cat(paste0("  KS test p-value: ", round(ks$p.value, 4), "\n"))

    abs(mean(samples) - 3) < 0.3 && ks$p.value > 0.01
  })

  # ---- Sampling Tests (Beta) ----
  cat("--- Beta Distribution Tests ---\n\n")

  run_test("test_beta_sampling", {
    cat("  Sampling 3000 points from Beta(2, 5) on [0.01, 0.99]...\n")
    set.seed(55)
    beta_dens <- function(x) dbeta(x, shape1 = 2, shape2 = 5)
    samples <- ars(beta_dens, c(0.01, 0.99), n = 3000)
    expected_mean <- 2 / (2 + 5)
    expected_sd <- sqrt(2 * 5 / ((2 + 5)^2 * (2 + 5 + 1)))
    cat(paste0("  Sample mean: ", round(mean(samples), 4),
               " (expected ~", round(expected_mean, 4), ")\n"))
    cat(paste0("  Sample sd:   ", round(sd(samples), 4),
               " (expected ~", round(expected_sd, 4), ")\n"))

    ks <- ks.test(samples, "pbeta", shape1 = 2, shape2 = 5)
    cat(paste0("  KS test p-value: ", round(ks$p.value, 4), "\n"))

    abs(mean(samples) - expected_mean) < 0.05 && ks$p.value > 0.01
  })

  # ---- Sampling Tests (Log-Normal via unnormalized density) ----
  cat("--- Unnormalized Density Test ---\n\n")

  run_test("test_unnormalized_density", {
    cat("  Sampling from unnormalized Gaussian: f(x) = exp(-x^2/2)...\n")
    set.seed(33)
    unnorm_gauss <- function(x) exp(-x^2 / 2)
    samples <- ars(unnorm_gauss, n = 3000, lb = -Inf, ub = Inf)
    cat(paste0("  Sample mean: ", round(mean(samples), 4), " (expected ~0)\n"))
    cat(paste0("  Sample sd:   ", round(sd(samples), 4), " (expected ~1)\n"))

    ks <- ks.test(samples, "pnorm")
    cat(paste0("  KS test p-value: ", round(ks$p.value, 4), "\n"))

    abs(mean(samples)) < 0.15 && abs(sd(samples) - 1) < 0.15 && ks$p.value > 0.01
  })

  # ---- Module-Level Tests ----
  cat("--- Module-Level Tests ---\n\n")

  run_test("test_eval_log_density", {
    cat("  Testing eval_log_density with dnorm...\n")
    result <- eval_log_density(dnorm, 0)
    expected <- log(dnorm(0))
    cat(paste0("  log(dnorm(0)) = ", round(result, 6), " (expected ", round(expected, 6), ")\n"))
    abs(result - expected) < 1e-10
  })

  run_test("test_check_log_concavity_valid", {
    cat("  Testing log-concavity check on concave data...\n")
    # h(x) = -x^2 is concave
    x <- c(-2, -1, 0, 1, 2)
    h <- -x^2
    result <- tryCatch({ check_log_concavity(x, h); TRUE },
                       error = function(e) FALSE)
    result
  })

  run_test("test_check_log_concavity_invalid", {
    cat("  Testing log-concavity check on convex data...\n")
    # h(x) = x^2 is convex (NOT concave)
    x <- c(-2, -1, 0, 1, 2)
    h <- x^2
    result <- tryCatch({ check_log_concavity(x, h); FALSE },
                       error = function(e) grepl("[Ll]og-concav", e$message))
    result
  })

  run_test("test_upper_hull_above_density", {
    cat("  Testing that upper hull is above log-density at test points...\n")
    set.seed(11)
    x_abs <- c(-3, -1, 0, 1, 3)
    h_abs <- eval_log_density(dnorm, x_abs)
    hull <- compute_upper_hull(x_abs, h_abs, -Inf, Inf)

    # Check at many random points
    test_pts <- seq(-2.5, 2.5, by = 0.1)
    h_test <- eval_log_density(dnorm, test_pts)
    u_test <- eval_upper_hull(test_pts, hull)

    all_above <- all(u_test >= h_test - 1e-10)
    cat(paste0("  Upper hull >= log-density at all ", length(test_pts), " test points: ", all_above, "\n"))
    all_above
  })

  run_test("test_lower_hull_below_density", {
    cat("  Testing that lower hull is below log-density at test points...\n")
    x_abs <- c(-3, -1, 0, 1, 3)
    h_abs <- eval_log_density(dnorm, x_abs)

    test_pts <- seq(-2.5, 2.5, by = 0.1)
    h_test <- eval_log_density(dnorm, test_pts)
    l_test <- sapply(test_pts, function(x) eval_lower_hull(x, x_abs, h_abs))

    all_below <- all(l_test <= h_test + 1e-10)
    cat(paste0("  Lower hull <= log-density at all ", length(test_pts), " test points: ", all_below, "\n"))
    all_below
  })

  # ---- Summary ----
  cat("============================================================\n")
  cat(paste0("  TOTAL: ", pass_count + fail_count, " tests, ",
             pass_count, " passed, ", fail_count, " failed\n"))
  cat("============================================================\n")

  invisible(list(passed = pass_count, failed = fail_count))
}

# Run tests if this script is executed directly
if (sys.nframe() == 0) {
  test()
}

steps.reasoning_content:
The error "'vec' must be sorted non-decreasingly and not contain NAs" comes from `findInterval`. This is happening in my upper hull when the breakpoints `z` contain NAs or are not sorted. This probably happens when the secant slopes are equal (division by zero) or when the breakpoints are not monotonically increasing.

Let me debug this. The issue is in `compute_upper_hull` when computing breakpoints. If two adjacent secant slopes are equal, we get division by zero. Also, the breakpoints might not be in order.

Let me add some debugging to understand the issue better. The bounded case [-5, 5] and unnormalized Gaussian work (finite bounds or happens to avoid the issue), but exponential on [0.001, 100] and normal on (-Inf, Inf) fail.


I'll add some debug output to trace what's happening with the breakpoints and slopes.

steps.reasoning_content:
I see the issue! The breakpoints include `-1, -1` (duplicates) because the left and right arms of the tent at x=-1 meet at x=-1 itself. The `z` vector has repeated values: `-Inf -1 -1 -0.6 0 0.6 1 1 Inf`. The `findInterval` function expects strictly increasing values... actually, `findInterval` should handle ties, but the issue might be with NaN values from other cases.

Wait, let me check for the exponential case. The issue is with initial abscissae at [0.001, 100].


The slopes are decreasing as expected, and the z breakpoints are non-decreasing, which `findInterval` should handle fine. The problem must be something else—maybe the function is cutting off mid-check.

Let me trace through the exponential case more carefully. For the interval [0.001, 100], the initial abscissae start with `lb` plus a sequence of offsets.

The log-density of an exponential distribution is just `-x`, which means all the secant slopes between consecutive points are constant at -1. When I try to find where two lines with identical slopes intersect, the denominator becomes zero and I get NaN—that's where the error is coming from.

The normal distribution has a similar problem: the initial points are evenly spaced from -4 to 4, which creates symmetric pairs with equal and opposite slopes that also lead to division by zero in the intersection calculation. The secant slopes between adjacent points should be approximately -x, so they're all distinct. But when computing the upper hull breakpoints, if two adjacent segments end up with nearly identical slopes, the denominator becomes zero or near-zero, producing NaN or Inf values. This is especially problematic for distributions like the exponential where all secant slopes are constant at -1, causing parallel segments in the upper hull.

When parallel lines don't coincide, one is always above the other, so the intersection point needs special handling rather than the standard formula.

For the exponential case where all slopes equal -1, the tent at each point is actually just the function itself since left and right slopes match. The breakpoints fall exactly at the sample points, and I can verify this by writing out the line equations—consecutive segments through adjacent points both have slope -1, so they're parallel lines that only "meet" at their shared endpoints.

The real issue is detecting when slopes are equal and handling that case properly. When `seg_m[i] == seg_m[i+1]`, I need to check if the intercepts also match—if they do, it's the same line and I can place the breakpoint at the midpoint between those abscissae. If the intercepts differ, one line dominates and I can use any point in between.

For the exponential case specifically, since the log-density is linear, the upper hull, lower hull, and true function all coincide, so the squeeze test will always accept and I just need to sample from the exponential distribution. The key is fixing the breakpoint computation to handle equal slopes by using the midpoint of the segment reference points.

I should also verify that breakpoints are naturally sorted for a log-concave distribution's upper hull—if they're not, that signals a deeper issue. Let me trace through a concrete exponential example to check this.

For dexp on [0.001, 100] with test points [0.101, 0.501, 1.001, 2.001, 3.001], all secant slopes equal -1, which means each abscissa generates two hull segments (left and right arms) with the same slope, creating a series of overlapping segments at each point.

The issue is that when slopes are identical, the breakpoint calculation produces NaN because the denominator becomes zero. The solution is to set breakpoints at the shared reference point or midpoint when consecutive segments have equal slopes, and I should also consider merging identical consecutive segments into a single line rather than keeping them separate.

steps.message: The issue is that when the log-density is linear (like exponential), all secant slopes are identical, causing division by zero in the breakpoint computation. Let me fix the upper hull to handle this.

results.content:
1→###############################################################################
     2→# Adaptive Rejection Sampling (ARS)
     3→# Based on Gilks, W.R. & Wild, P. (1992)
     4→# "Adaptive Rejection Sampling for Gibbs Sampling"
     5→# Journal of the Royal Statistical Society: Series C, 41(2), 337-348.
     6→#
     7→# Derivative-free implementation using secant-based upper hull construction.
     8→###############################################################################
     9→
    10→# --- Auxiliary / Module Functions ---
    11→
    12→#' Evaluate the log of a density function safely
    13→#' @param f density function (possibly unnormalized)
    14→#' @param x point(s) at which to evaluate
    15→#' @return log(f(x)), with protection against f(x) <= 0
    16→eval_log_density <- function(f, x) {
    17→  fx <- f(x)
    18→  if (any(is.na(fx) | is.nan(fx))) {
    19→    stop("Density function returned NA/NaN.")
    20→  }
    21→  if (any(fx < 0)) {
    22→    stop("Density function returned negative value.")
    23→  }
    24→  result <- rep(-Inf, length(fx))
    25→  pos <- fx > 0
    26→  result[pos] <- log(fx[pos])
    27→  return(result)
    28→}
    29→
    30→#' Check log-concavity at three consecutive abscissae
    31→#' @param abscissae sorted vector of x values
    32→#' @param h_vals corresponding log-density values h(x)
    33→#' @return TRUE if log-concave, else stops with error
    34→check_log_concavity <- function(abscissae, h_vals) {
    35→  n <- length(abscissae)
    36→  if (n < 3) return(TRUE)
    37→  for (i in 1:(n - 2)) {
    38→    x1 <- abscissae[i];   h1 <- h_vals[i]
    39→    x2 <- abscissae[i+1]; h2 <- h_vals[i+1]
    40→    x3 <- abscissae[i+2]; h3 <- h_vals[i+2]
    41→    slope_left  <- (h2 - h1) / (x2 - x1)
    42→    slope_right <- (h3 - h2) / (x3 - x2)
    43→    if (slope_right > slope_left + 1e-10) {
    44→      stop(paste0(
    45→        "Log-concavity violated at x = ", x2,
    46→        ". Left slope = ", round(slope_left, 6),
    47→        ", Right slope = ", round(slope_right, 6),
    48→        ". The density may not be log-concave."
    49→      ))
    50→    }
    51→  }
    52→  return(TRUE)
    53→}
    54→
    55→#' Compute the upper hull for the ARS algorithm (derivative-free version)
    56→#'
    57→#' For k abscissae with log-density values, the upper hull is piecewise linear
    58→#' and lies above the log-density everywhere. It is constructed from the secant
    59→#' slopes between consecutive abscissae. By log-concavity, secant slopes
    60→#' decrease: s_1 > s_2 > ... > s_{k-1}.
    61→#'
    62→#' At each interior abscissa x_j, the upper hull has a "tent" with:
    63→#'   - left arm:  slope s_j (right secant), valid for x <= x_j
    64→#'   - right arm: slope s_{j-1} (left secant), valid for x >= x_j
    65→#'
    66→#' @param abscissae sorted x values (at least 3)
    67→#' @param h_vals corresponding h(x) = log(f(x)) values
    68→#' @param lb lower bound of domain
    69→#' @param ub upper bound of domain
    70→#' @return list with segment info for the piecewise linear upper hull
    71→compute_upper_hull <- function(abscissae, h_vals, lb, ub) {
    72→  k <- length(abscissae)
    73→  if (k < 3) stop("Need at least 3 abscissae for upper hull construction.")
    74→
    75→  # Secant slopes: s[j] = slope from x_j to x_{j+1}
    76→  s <- diff(h_vals) / diff(abscissae)
    77→
    78→  # Build the list of line segments for the upper hull.
    79→  # Each segment is defined by a point (px, ph) and slope m:
    80→  #   u(x) = ph + m * (x - px) = (ph - m*px) + m*x
    81→  #
    82→  # Segments from left to right:
    83→  # 1. x_1's left arm: slope s[1], through (x_1, h_1), for x from lb to z_1
    84→  # Then for each interior point j = 2,...,k-1:
    85→  #   left arm:  slope s[j], through (x_j, h_j)
    86→  #   right arm: slope s[j-1], through (x_j, h_j)
    87→  # Last: x_k's right arm: slope s[k-1], through (x_k, h_k), for x from z_{k-1} to ub
    88→
    89→  n_seg <- 2 * (k - 1)
    90→  seg_px <- numeric(n_seg)
    91→  seg_ph <- numeric(n_seg)
    92→  seg_m  <- numeric(n_seg)
    93→
    94→  idx <- 1
    95→  # First segment: x_1, slope s[1]
    96→  seg_px[idx] <- abscissae[1]; seg_ph[idx] <- h_vals[1]; seg_m[idx] <- s[1]
    97→  idx <- idx + 1
    98→
    99→  # Interior points
   100→  for (j in 2:(k-1)) {
   101→    # Left arm: slope s[j]
   102→    seg_px[idx] <- abscissae[j]; seg_ph[idx] <- h_vals[j]; seg_m[idx] <- s[j]
   103→    idx <- idx + 1
   104→    # Right arm: slope s[j-1]
   105→    seg_px[idx] <- abscissae[j]; seg_ph[idx] <- h_vals[j]; seg_m[idx] <- s[j-1]
   106→    idx <- idx + 1
   107→  }
   108→
   109→  # Last segment: x_k, slope s[k-1]
   110→  seg_px[idx] <- abscissae[k]; seg_ph[idx] <- h_vals[k]; seg_m[idx] <- s[k-1]
   111→
   112→  # Convert to intercept form: u(x) = intercept + slope * x
   113→  seg_int <- seg_ph - seg_m * seg_px
   114→
   115→  # Compute breakpoints between consecutive segments
   116→  bp <- numeric(n_seg - 1)
   117→  for (i in 1:(n_seg - 1)) {
   118→    if (seg_px[i] == seg_px[i+1] && seg_ph[i] == seg_ph[i+1]) {
   119→      # Same point (left/right arm of same tent): breakpoint is at that x
   120→      bp[i] <- seg_px[i]
   121→    } else {
   122→      # Intersection of two different lines
   123→      bp[i] <- (seg_int[i+1] - seg_int[i]) / (seg_m[i] - seg_m[i+1])
   124→    }
   125→  }
   126→
   127→  z <- c(lb, bp, ub)
   128→
   129→  return(list(
   130→    z = z,
   131→    seg_slopes = seg_m,
   132→    seg_intercepts = seg_int,
   133→    n_seg = n_seg
   134→  ))
   135→}
   136→
   137→#' Evaluate the upper hull at a point x
   138→#' @param x scalar or vector of points
   139→#' @param hull list from compute_upper_hull
   140→#' @return u(x), the upper hull value(s)
   141→eval_upper_hull <- function(x, hull) {
   142→  z <- hull$z
   143→  m <- hull$seg_slopes
   144→  b <- hull$seg_intercepts
   145→  n_seg <- hull$n_seg
   146→
   147→  seg_idx <- findInterval(x, z, rightmost.closed = TRUE)
   148→  seg_idx <- pmax(1, pmin(seg_idx, n_seg))
   149→
   150→  return(b[seg_idx] + m[seg_idx] * x)
   151→}
   152→
   153→#' Evaluate the lower hull (piecewise linear through abscissae) at a point x
   154→#' @param x scalar point
   155→#' @param abscissae sorted x values
   156→#' @param h_vals corresponding h(x) values
   157→#' @return l(x), the lower hull value. -Inf if outside the range of abscissae.
   158→eval_lower_hull <- function(x, abscissae, h_vals) {
   159→  k <- length(abscissae)
   160→  if (x <= abscissae[1] || x >= abscissae[k]) {
   161→    return(-Inf)
   162→  }
   163→  j <- findInterval(x, abscissae)
   164→  j <- pmax(1, pmin(j, k - 1))
   165→  t <- (x - abscissae[j]) / (abscissae[j+1] - abscissae[j])
   166→  return(h_vals[j] * (1 - t) + h_vals[j+1] * t)
   167→}
   168→
   169→#' Sample from the upper hull (piecewise-exponential distribution)
   170→#' @param n number of samples to draw
   171→#' @param hull list from compute_upper_hull
   172→#' @return vector of n samples
   173→sample_upper_hull <- function(n, hull) {
   174→  z <- hull$z
   175→  m <- hull$seg_slopes
   176→  b <- hull$seg_intercepts
   177→  n_seg <- hull$n_seg
   178→
   179→  # Compute area under each segment: integral of exp(b + m*x) from z[j] to z[j+1]
   180→  areas <- numeric(n_seg)
   181→  for (j in 1:n_seg) {
   182→    lo <- z[j]
   183→    hi <- z[j + 1]
   184→
   185→    # Handle infinite bounds: for the integral to converge, slope must be favorable
   186→    if (!is.finite(lo)) {
   187→      # lo = -Inf. Need m[j] > 0 for convergence
   188→      if (m[j] <= 0) {
   189→        areas[j] <- 0
   190→        next
   191→      }
   192→      # Integral of exp(b + m*x) from -Inf to hi = exp(b + m*hi) / m
   193→      areas[j] <- exp(b[j] + m[j] * hi) / m[j]
   194→      next
   195→    }
   196→    if (!is.finite(hi)) {
   197→      # hi = +Inf. Need m[j] < 0 for convergence
   198→      if (m[j] >= 0) {
   199→        areas[j] <- 0
   200→        next
   201→      }
   202→      # Integral of exp(b + m*x) from lo to Inf = -exp(b + m*lo) / m
   203→      areas[j] <- -exp(b[j] + m[j] * lo) / m[j]
   204→      next
   205→    }
   206→
   207→    if (abs(m[j]) < 1e-15) {
   208→      areas[j] <- exp(b[j]) * (hi - lo)
   209→    } else {
   210→      # Integral = (exp(b + m*hi) - exp(b + m*lo)) / m
   211→      # For numerical stability, factor out the larger value
   212→      log_hi <- b[j] + m[j] * hi
   213→      log_lo <- b[j] + m[j] * lo
   214→      log_max <- max(log_hi, log_lo)
   215→      areas[j] <- exp(log_max) * (exp(log_hi - log_max) - exp(log_lo - log_max)) / m[j]
   216→    }
   217→
   218→    if (is.na(areas[j]) || !is.finite(areas[j]) || areas[j] < 0) {
   219→      areas[j] <- 0
   220→    }
   221→  }
   222→
   223→  total_area <- sum(areas)
   224→  if (total_area <= 0 || !is.finite(total_area)) {
   225→    stop("Upper hull has zero or infinite area. Check domain bounds and initial abscissae.")
   226→  }
   227→  probs <- areas / total_area
   228→
   229→  # Sample segments according to their areas
   230→  seg_choices <- sample.int(n_seg, size = n, replace = TRUE, prob = probs)
   231→
   232→  # For each chosen segment, sample from the truncated exponential via inverse CDF
   233→  samples <- numeric(n)
   234→  for (i in 1:n) {
   235→    j <- seg_choices[i]
   236→    lo <- z[j]
   237→    hi <- z[j + 1]
   238→    u <- runif(1)
   239→
   240→    if (abs(m[j]) < 1e-15) {
   241→      # Uniform on [lo, hi]
   242→      if (!is.finite(lo)) lo <- hi - 20
   243→      if (!is.finite(hi)) hi <- lo + 20
   244→      samples[i] <- lo + u * (hi - lo)
   245→    } else if (!is.finite(lo)) {
   246→      # lo = -Inf, m > 0: CDF(x) = exp(m*(x-hi)) for x <= hi
   247→      # Inverse: x = hi + log(u) / m
   248→      samples[i] <- hi + log(u) / m[j]
   249→    } else if (!is.finite(hi)) {
   250→      # hi = +Inf, m < 0: CDF(x) = 1 - exp(m*(x-lo)) for x >= lo
   251→      # Inverse: x = lo + log(1 - u) / m  ... but log(1-u) ~ log(u) in distribution
   252→      samples[i] <- lo + log(u) / m[j]  # since m < 0, this gives x > lo
   253→    } else {
   254→      # Both finite: inverse CDF of truncated exponential
   255→      log_lo <- m[j] * lo
   256→      log_hi <- m[j] * hi
   257→      # CDF: F(x) = (exp(m*x) - exp(m*lo)) / (exp(m*hi) - exp(m*lo))
   258→      # Inverse: x = log(exp(m*lo) + u*(exp(m*hi) - exp(m*lo))) / m
   259→      log_max <- max(log_lo, log_hi)
   260→      exp_lo <- exp(log_lo - log_max)
   261→      exp_hi <- exp(log_hi - log_max)
   262→      samples[i] <- (log_max + log(exp_lo + u * (exp_hi - exp_lo))) / m[j]
   263→    }
   264→  }
   265→  return(samples)
   266→}
   267→
   268→#' Choose initial abscissae for the ARS algorithm
   269→#' Ensures at least 3 points with positive density, and that
   270→#' boundary slopes are compatible with infinite domains.
   271→#' @param lb lower bound
   272→#' @param ub upper bound
   273→#' @param f density function
   274→#' @param n_init number of initial points (default 5, minimum 3)
   275→#' @return vector of initial abscissae
   276→choose_initial_abscissae <- function(lb, ub, f, n_init = 20) {
   277→  n_init <- max(n_init, 5)
   278→
   279→  # Generate candidate points
   280→  if (is.finite(lb) && is.finite(ub)) {
   281→    margin <- (ub - lb) * 0.02
   282→    pts <- seq(lb + margin, ub - margin, length.out = n_init)
   283→  } else if (is.finite(lb) && !is.finite(ub)) {
   284→    pts <- lb + c(0.1, 0.5, 1, 2, 3, 5, 8, 12, 18, 25, 35, 50,
   285→                  0.2, 0.8, 1.5, 4, 7, 10, 15, 20)[1:n_init]
   286→  } else if (!is.finite(lb) && is.finite(ub)) {
   287→    pts <- ub - c(0.1, 0.5, 1, 2, 3, 5, 8, 12, 18, 25, 35, 50,
   288→                  0.2, 0.8, 1.5, 4, 7, 10, 15, 20)[1:n_init]
   289→  } else {
   290→    # Both infinite: center around 0
   291→    pts <- c(seq(-4, 4, length.out = n_init))
   292→  }
   293→
   294→  pts <- sort(unique(pts))
   295→
   296→  # Keep only points with positive density
   297→  vals <- f(pts)
   298→  good <- which(vals > 0 & !is.na(vals) & is.finite(vals))
   299→  if (length(good) < 3) {
   300→    stop("Cannot find at least 3 initial abscissae with positive density. Check domain bounds.")
   301→  }
   302→  pts <- pts[good]
   303→
   304→  # Compute log-density
   305→  h <- log(vals[good])
   306→
   307→  # For infinite left bound: need first secant slope > 0
   308→  # For infinite right bound: need last secant slope < 0
   309→  # Trim from each end until this is satisfied
   310→  if (!is.finite(lb)) {
   311→    while (length(pts) > 3) {
   312→      s1 <- (h[2] - h[1]) / (pts[2] - pts[1])
   313→      if (s1 > 0) break
   314→      pts <- pts[-1]; h <- h[-1]
   315→    }
   316→  }
   317→  if (!is.finite(ub)) {
   318→    while (length(pts) > 3) {
   319→      k <- length(pts)
   320→      sk <- (h[k] - h[k-1]) / (pts[k] - pts[k-1])
   321→      if (sk < 0) break
   322→      pts <- pts[-k]; h <- h[-k]
   323→    }
   324→  }
   325→
   326→  if (length(pts) < 3) {
   327→    stop("Cannot find suitable initial abscissae. The density mode may be outside the initial search range.")
   328→  }
   329→
   330→  return(pts)
   331→}
   332→
   333→#' Validate inputs to the ars function
   334→#' @param f density function
   335→#' @param n number of samples
   336→#' @param lb lower bound
   337→#' @param ub upper bound
   338→validate_inputs <- function(f, n, lb, ub) {
   339→  if (!is.function(f)) {
   340→    stop("'f' must be a function.")
   341→  }
   342→  if (!is.numeric(n) || length(n) != 1 || n < 1 || n != as.integer(n)) {
   343→    stop("'n' must be a positive integer.")
   344→  }
   345→  if (!is.numeric(lb) || length(lb) != 1) {
   346→    stop("'lb' must be a single numeric value.")
   347→  }
   348→  if (!is.numeric(ub) || length(ub) != 1) {
   349→    stop("'ub' must be a single numeric value.")
   350→  }
   351→  if (lb >= ub) {
   352→    stop("Lower bound must be strictly less than upper bound.")
   353→  }
   354→}
   355→
   356→# --- Main ARS Function ---
   357→
   358→#' Adaptive Rejection Sampling
   359→#'
   360→#' @param f density function (possibly unnormalized), must be log-concave.
   361→#'          Should accept a vector and return a vector of density values.
   362→#' @param support optional 2-element vector c(lb, ub) for the domain
   363→#' @param n number of samples to generate (default 100)
   364→#' @param lb lower bound of the domain (default -Inf)
   365→#' @param ub upper bound of the domain (default Inf)
   366→#' @param ... additional arguments (currently unused)
   367→#' @return vector of n samples from the target distribution
   368→ars <- function(f, support = NULL, n = 100, lb = -Inf, ub = Inf, ...) {
   369→  # Handle the support vector argument (alternative call pattern)
   370→  if (!is.null(support) && is.numeric(support) && length(support) == 2) {
   371→    lb <- support[1]
   372→    ub <- support[2]
   373→  }
   374→
   375→  # Validate inputs
   376→  validate_inputs(f, n, lb, ub)
   377→
   378→  # Choose initial abscissae (at least 3)
   379→  abscissae <- choose_initial_abscissae(lb, ub, f)
   380→  h_vals <- eval_log_density(f, abscissae)
   381→
   382→  # Check initial log-concavity
   383→  check_log_concavity(abscissae, h_vals)
   384→
   385→  # Main sampling loop
   386→  samples <- numeric(n)
   387→  count <- 0
   388→  max_iter <- n * 200  # safety limit
   389→  iter <- 0
   390→
   391→  while (count < n && iter < max_iter) {
   392→    iter <- iter + 1
   393→
   394→    # Build upper hull
   395→    hull <- compute_upper_hull(abscissae, h_vals, lb, ub)
   396→
   397→    # Sample a candidate from the upper hull
   398→    x_star <- sample_upper_hull(1, hull)
   399→
   400→    # Squeeze and rejection test
   401→    u <- runif(1)
   402→    u_val <- eval_upper_hull(x_star, hull)
   403→    l_val <- eval_lower_hull(x_star, abscissae, h_vals)
   404→
   405→    if (u <= exp(l_val - u_val)) {
   406→      # Accept by squeeze test (no density evaluation needed)
   407→      count <- count + 1
   408→      samples[count] <- x_star
   409→    } else {
   410→      # Need to evaluate the true log-density
   411→      h_star <- eval_log_density(f, x_star)
   412→
   413→      if (u <= exp(h_star - u_val)) {
   414→        # Accept by rejection test
   415→        count <- count + 1
   416→        samples[count] <- x_star
   417→      }
   418→
   419→      # Update abscissae with new point (whether accepted or not)
   420→      insert_pos <- findInterval(x_star, abscissae) + 1
   421→      abscissae <- append(abscissae, x_star, after = insert_pos - 1)
   422→      h_vals <- append(h_vals, h_star, after = insert_pos - 1)
   423→
   424→      # Re-check log-concavity with new point
   425→      check_log_concavity(abscissae, h_vals)
   426→    }
   427→  }
   428→
   429→  if (count < n) {
   430→    warning(paste("Only generated", count, "out of", n, "requested samples."))
   431→  }
   432→
   433→  return(samples)
   434→}
   435→
   436→# --- Test Function ---

   440→#' Output format: "TEST_NAME: PASS" or "TEST_NAME: FAIL"
   441→test <- function() {

[metadata] {"type": "text", "file": {"filePath": "/app/ars.R", "content": "###############################################################################\n# Adaptive Rejection Sampling (ARS)\n# Based on Gilks, W.R. & Wild, P. (1992)\n# \"Adaptive Rejection Sampling for Gibbs Sampling\"\n# Journal of the Royal Statistical Society: Series C, 41(2), 337-348.\n#\n# Derivative-free implementation using secant-based upper hull construction.\n###############################################################################\n\n# --- Auxiliary / Module Functions ---\n\n#' Evaluate the log of a density function safely\n#' @param f density function (possibly unnormalized)\n#' @param x point(s) at which to evaluate\n#' @return log(f(x)), with protection against f(x) <= 0\neval_log_density <- function(f, x) {\n  fx <- f(x)\n  if (any(is.na(fx) | is.nan(fx))) {\n    stop(\"Density function returned NA/NaN.\")\n  }\n  if (any(fx < 0)) {\n    stop(\"Density function returned negative value.\")\n  }\n  result <- rep(-Inf, length(fx))\n  pos <- fx > 0\n  result[pos] <- log(fx[pos])\n  return(result)\n}\n\n#' Check log-concavity at three consecutive abscissae\n#' @param abscissae sorted vector of x values\n#' @param h_vals corresponding log-density values h(x)\n#' @return TRUE if log-concave, else stops with error\ncheck_log_concavity <- function(abscissae, h_vals) {\n  n <- length(abscissae)\n  if (n < 3) return(TRUE)\n  for (i in 1:(n - 2)) {\n    x1 <- abscissae[i];   h1 <- h_vals[i]\n    x2 <- abscissae[i+1]; h2 <- h_vals[i+1]\n    x3 <- abscissae[i+2]; h3 <- h_vals[i+2]\n    slope_left  <- (h2 - h1) / (x2 - x1)\n    slope_right <- (h3 - h2) / (x3 - x2)\n    if (slope_right > slope_left + 1e-10) {\n      stop(paste0(\n        \"Log-concavity violated at x = \", x2,\n        \". Left slope = \", round(slope_left, 6),\n        \", Right slope = \", round(slope_right, 6),\n        \". The density may not be log-concave.\"\n      ))\n    }\n  }\n  return(TRUE)\n}\n\n#' Compute the upper hull for the ARS algorithm (derivative-free version)\n#'\n#' For k abscissae with log-density values, the upper hull is piecewise linear\n#' and lies above the log-density everywhere. It is constructed from the secant\n#' slopes between consecutive abscissae. By log-concavity, secant slopes\n#' decrease: s_1 > s_2 > ... > s_{k-1}.\n#'\n#' At each interior abscissa x_j, the upper hull has a \"tent\" with:\n#'   - left arm:  slope s_j (right secant), valid for x <= x_j\n#'   - right arm: slope s_{j-1} (left secant), valid for x >= x_j\n#'\n#' @param abscissae sorted x values (at least 3)\n#' @param h_vals corresponding h(x) = log(f(x)) values\n#' @param lb lower bound of domain\n#' @param ub upper bound of domain\n#' @return list with segment info for the piecewise linear upper hull\ncompute_upper_hull <- function(abscissae, h_vals, lb, ub) {\n  k <- length(abscissae)\n  if (k < 3) stop(\"Need at least 3 abscissae for upper hull construction.\")\n\n  # Secant slopes: s[j] = slope from x_j to x_{j+1}\n  s <- diff(h_vals) / diff(abscissae)\n\n  # Build the list of line segments for the upper hull.\n  # Each segment is defined by a point (px, ph) and slope m:\n  #   u(x) = ph + m * (x - px) = (ph - m*px) + m*x\n  #\n  # Segments from left to right:\n  # 1. x_1's left arm: slope s[1], through (x_1, h_1), for x from lb to z_1\n  # Then for each interior point j = 2,...,k-1:\n  #   left arm:  slope s[j], through (x_j, h_j)\n  #   right arm: slope s[j-1], through (x_j, h_j)\n  # Last: x_k's right arm: slope s[k-1], through (x_k, h_k), for x from z_{k-1} to ub\n\n  n_seg <- 2 * (k - 1)\n  seg_px <- numeric(n_seg)\n  seg_ph <- numeric(n_seg)\n  seg_m  <- numeric(n_seg)\n\n  idx <- 1\n  # First segment: x_1, slope s[1]\n  seg_px[idx] <- abscissae[1]; seg_ph[idx] <- h_vals[1]; seg_m[idx] <- s[1]\n  idx <- idx + 1\n\n  # Interior points\n  for (j in 2:(k-1)) {\n    # Left arm: slope s[j]\n    seg_px[idx] <- abscissae[j]; seg_ph[idx] <- h_vals[j]; seg_m[idx] <- s[j]\n    idx <- idx + 1\n    # Right arm: slope s[j-1]\n    seg_px[idx] <- abscissae[j]; seg_ph[idx] <- h_vals[j]; seg_m[idx] <- s[j-1]\n    idx <- idx + 1\n  }\n\n  # Last segment: x_k, slope s[k-1]\n  seg_px[idx] <- abscissae[k]; seg_ph[idx] <- h_vals[k]; seg_m[idx] <- s[k-1]\n\n  # Convert to intercept form: u(x) = intercept + slope * x\n  seg_int <- seg_ph - seg_m * seg_px\n\n  # Compute breakpoints between consecutive segments\n  bp <- numeric(n_seg - 1)\n  for (i in 1:(n_seg - 1)) {\n    if (seg_px[i] == seg_px[i+1] && seg_ph[i] == seg_ph[i+1]) {\n      # Same point (left/right arm of same tent): breakpoint is at that x\n      bp[i] <- seg_px[i]\n    } else {\n      # Intersection of two different lines\n      bp[i] <- (seg_int[i+1] - seg_int[i]) / (seg_m[i] - seg_m[i+1])\n    }\n  }\n\n  z <- c(lb, bp, ub)\n\n  return(list(\n    z = z,\n    seg_slopes = seg_m,\n    seg_intercepts = seg_int,\n    n_seg = n_seg\n  ))\n}\n\n#' Evaluate the upper hull at a point x\n#' @param x scalar or vector of points\n#' @param hull list from compute_upper_hull\n#' @return u(x), the upper hull value(s)\neval_upper_hull <- function(x, hull) {\n  z <- hull$z\n  m <- hull$seg_slopes\n  b <- hull$seg_intercepts\n  n_seg <- hull$n_seg\n\n  seg_idx <- findInterval(x, z, rightmost.closed = TRUE)\n  seg_idx <- pmax(1, pmin(seg_idx, n_seg))\n\n  return(b[seg_idx] + m[seg_idx] * x)\n}\n\n#' Evaluate the lower hull (piecewise linear through abscissae) at a point x\n#' @param x scalar point\n#' @param abscissae sorted x values\n#' @param h_vals corresponding h(x) values\n#' @return l(x), the lower hull value. -Inf if outside the range of abscissae.\neval_lower_hull <- function(x, abscissae, h_vals) {\n  k <- length(abscissae)\n  if (x <= abscissae[1] || x >= abscissae[k]) {\n    return(-Inf)\n  }\n  j <- findInterval(x, abscissae)\n  j <- pmax(1, pmin(j, k - 1))\n  t <- (x - abscissae[j]) / (abscissae[j+1] - abscissae[j])\n  return(h_vals[j] * (1 - t) + h_vals[j+1] * t)\n}\n\n#' Sample from the upper hull (piecewise-exponential distribution)\n#' @param n number of samples to draw\n#' @param hull list from compute_upper_hull\n#' @return vector of n samples\nsample_upper_hull <- function(n, hull) {\n  z <- hull$z\n  m <- hull$seg_slopes\n  b <- hull$seg_intercepts\n  n_seg <- hull$n_seg\n\n  # Compute area under each segment: integral of exp(b + m*x) from z[j] to z[j+1]\n  areas <- numeric(n_seg)\n  for (j in 1:n_seg) {\n    lo <- z[j]\n    hi <- z[j + 1]\n\n    # Handle infinite bounds: for the integral to converge, slope must be favorable\n    if (!is.finite(lo)) {\n      # lo = -Inf. Need m[j] > 0 for convergence\n      if (m[j] <= 0) {\n        areas[j] <- 0\n        next\n      }\n      # Integral of exp(b + m*x) from -Inf to hi = exp(b + m*hi) / m\n      areas[j] <- exp(b[j] + m[j] * hi) / m[j]\n      next\n    }\n    if (!is.finite(hi)) {\n      # hi = +Inf. Need m[j] < 0 for convergence\n      if (m[j] >= 0) {\n        areas[j] <- 0\n        next\n      }\n      # Integral of exp(b + m*x) from lo to Inf = -exp(b + m*lo) / m\n      areas[j] <- -exp(b[j] + m[j] * lo) / m[j]\n      next\n    }\n\n    if (abs(m[j]) < 1e-15) {\n      areas[j] <- exp(b[j]) * (hi - lo)\n    } else {\n      # Integral = (exp(b + m*hi) - exp(b + m*lo)) / m\n      # For numerical stability, factor out the larger value\n      log_hi <- b[j] + m[j] * hi\n      log_lo <- b[j] + m[j] * lo\n      log_max <- max(log_hi, log_lo)\n      areas[j] <- exp(log_max) * (exp(log_hi - log_max) - exp(log_lo - log_max)) / m[j]\n    }\n\n    if (is.na(areas[j]) || !is.finite(areas[j]) || areas[j] < 0) {\n      areas[j] <- 0\n    }\n  }\n\n  total_area <- sum(areas)\n  if (total_area <= 0 || !is.finite(total_area)) {\n    stop(\"Upper hull has zero or infinite area. Check domain bounds and initial abscissae.\")\n  }\n  probs <- areas / total_area\n\n  # Sample segments according to their areas\n  seg_choices <- sample.int(n_seg, size = n, replace = TRUE, prob = probs)\n\n  # For each chosen segment, sample from the truncated exponential via inverse CDF\n  samples <- numeric(n)\n  for (i in 1:n) {\n    j <- seg_choices[i]\n    lo <- z[j]\n    hi <- z[j + 1]\n    u <- runif(1)\n\n    if (abs(m[j]) < 1e-15) {\n      # Uniform on [lo, hi]\n      if (!is.finite(lo)) lo <- hi - 20\n      if (!is.finite(hi)) hi <- lo + 20\n      samples[i] <- lo + u * (hi - lo)\n    } else if (!is.finite(lo)) {\n      # lo = -Inf, m > 0: CDF(x) = exp(m*(x-hi)) for x <= hi\n      # Inverse: x = hi + log(u) / m\n      samples[i] <- hi + log(u) / m[j]\n    } else if (!is.finite(hi)) {\n      # hi = +Inf, m < 0: CDF(x) = 1 - exp(m*(x-lo)) for x >= lo\n      # Inverse: x = lo + log(1 - u) / m  ... but log(1-u) ~ log(u) in distribution\n      samples[i] <- lo + log(u) / m[j]  # since m < 0, this gives x > lo\n    } else {\n      # Both finite: inverse CDF of truncated exponential\n      log_lo <- m[j] * lo\n      log_hi <- m[j] * hi\n      # CDF: F(x) = (exp(m*x) - exp(m*lo)) / (exp(m*hi) - exp(m*lo))\n      # Inverse: x = log(exp(m*lo) + u*(exp(m*hi) - exp(m*lo))) / m\n      log_max <- max(log_lo, log_hi)\n      exp_lo <- exp(log_lo - log_max)\n      exp_hi <- exp(log_hi - log_max)\n      samples[i] <- (log_max + log(exp_lo + u * (exp_hi - exp_lo))) / m[j]\n    }\n  }\n  return(samples)\n}\n\n#' Choose initial abscissae for the ARS algorithm\n#' Ensures at least 3 points with positive density, and that\n#' boundary slopes are compatible with infinite domains.\n#' @param lb lower bound\n#' @param ub upper bound\n#' @param f density function\n#' @param n_init number of initial points (default 5, minimum 3)\n#' @return vector of initial abscissae\nchoose_initial_abscissae <- function(lb, ub, f, n_init = 20) {\n  n_init <- max(n_init, 5)\n\n  # Generate candidate points\n  if (is.finite(lb) && is.finite(ub)) {\n    margin <- (ub - lb) * 0.02\n    pts <- seq(lb + margin, ub - margin, length.out = n_init)\n  } else if (is.finite(lb) && !is.finite(ub)) {\n    pts <- lb + c(0.1, 0.5, 1, 2, 3, 5, 8, 12, 18, 25, 35, 50,\n                  0.2, 0.8, 1.5, 4, 7, 10, 15, 20)[1:n_init]\n  } else if (!is.finite(lb) && is.finite(ub)) {\n    pts <- ub - c(0.1, 0.5, 1, 2, 3, 5, 8, 12, 18, 25, 35, 50,\n                  0.2, 0.8, 1.5, 4, 7, 10, 15, 20)[1:n_init]\n  } else {\n    # Both infinite: center around 0\n    pts <- c(seq(-4, 4, length.out = n_init))\n  }\n\n  pts <- sort(unique(pts))\n\n  # Keep only points with positive density\n  vals <- f(pts)\n  good <- which(vals > 0 & !is.na(vals) & is.finite(vals))\n  if (length(good) < 3) {\n    stop(\"Cannot find at least 3 initial abscissae with positive density. Check domain bounds.\")\n  }\n  pts <- pts[good]\n\n  # Compute log-density\n  h <- log(vals[good])\n\n  # For infinite left bound: need first secant slope > 0\n  # For infinite right bound: need last secant slope < 0\n  # Trim from each end until this is satisfied\n  if (!is.finite(lb)) {\n    while (length(pts) > 3) {\n      s1 <- (h[2] - h[1]) / (pts[2] - pts[1])\n      if (s1 > 0) break\n      pts <- pts[-1]; h <- h[-1]\n    }\n  }\n  if (!is.finite(ub)) {\n    while (length(pts) > 3) {\n      k <- length(pts)\n      sk <- (h[k] - h[k-1]) / (pts[k] - pts[k-1])\n      if (sk < 0) break\n      pts <- pts[-k]; h <- h[-k]\n    }\n  }\n\n  if (length(pts) < 3) {\n    stop(\"Cannot find suitable initial abscissae. The density mode may be outside the initial search range.\")\n  }\n\n  return(pts)\n}\n\n#' Validate inputs to the ars function\n#' @param f density function\n#' @param n number of samples\n#' @param lb lower bound\n#' @param ub upper bound\nvalidate_inputs <- function(f, n, lb, ub) {\n  if (!is.function(f)) {\n    stop(\"'f' must be a function.\")\n  }\n  if (!is.numeric(n) || length(n) != 1 || n < 1 || n != as.integer(n)) {\n    stop(\"'n' must be a positive integer.\")\n  }\n  if (!is.numeric(lb) || length(lb) != 1) {\n    stop(\"'lb' must be a single numeric value.\")\n  }\n  if (!is.numeric(ub) || length(ub) != 1) {\n    stop(\"'ub' must be a single numeric value.\")\n  }\n  if (lb >= ub) {\n    stop(\"Lower bound must be strictly less than upper bound.\")\n  }\n}\n\n# --- Main ARS Function ---\n\n#' Adaptive Rejection Sampling\n#'\n#' @param f density function (possibly unnormalized), must be log-concave.\n#'          Should accept a vector and return a vector of density values.\n#' @param support optional 2-element vector c(lb, ub) for the domain\n#' @param n number of samples to generate (default 100)\n#' @param lb lower bound of the domain (default -Inf)\n#' @param ub upper bound of the domain (default Inf)\n#' @param ... additional arguments (currently unused)\n#' @return vector of n samples from the target distribution\nars <- function(f, support = NULL, n = 100, lb = -Inf, ub = Inf, ...) {\n  # Handle the support vector argument (alternative call pattern)\n  if (!is.null(support) && is.numeric(support) && length(support) == 2) {\n    lb <- support[1]\n    ub <- support[2]\n  }\n\n  # Validate inputs\n  validate_inputs(f, n, lb, ub)\n\n  # Choose initial abscissae (at least 3)\n  abscissae <- choose_initial_abscissae(lb, ub, f)\n  h_vals <- eval_log_density(f, abscissae)\n\n  # Check initial log-concavity\n  check_log_concavity(abscissae, h_vals)\n\n  # Main sampling loop\n  samples <- numeric(n)\n  count <- 0\n  max_iter <- n * 200  # safety limit\n  iter <- 0\n\n  while (count < n && iter < max_iter) {\n    iter <- iter + 1\n\n    # Build upper hull\n    hull <- compute_upper_hull(abscissae, h_vals, lb, ub)\n\n    # Sample a candidate from the upper hull\n    x_star <- sample_upper_hull(1, hull)\n\n    # Squeeze and rejection test\n    u <- runif(1)\n    u_val <- eval_upper_hull(x_star, hull)\n    l_val <- eval_lower_hull(x_star, abscissae, h_vals)\n\n    if (u <= exp(l_val - u_val)) {\n      # Accept by squeeze test (no density evaluation needed)\n      count <- count + 1\n      samples[count] <- x_star\n    } else {\n      # Need to evaluate the true log-density\n      h_star <- eval_log_density(f, x_star)\n\n      if (u <= exp(h_star - u_val)) {\n        # Accept by rejection test\n        count <- count + 1\n        samples[count] <- x_star\n      }\n\n      # Update abscissae with new point (whether accepted or not)\n      insert_pos <- findInterval(x_star, abscissae) + 1\n      abscissae <- append(abscissae, x_star, after = insert_pos - 1)\n      h_vals <- append(h_vals, h_star, after = insert_pos - 1)\n\n      # Re-check log-concavity with new point\n      check_log_concavity(abscissae, h_vals)\n    }\n  }\n\n  if (count < n) {\n    warning(paste(\"Only generated\", count, \"out of\", n, \"requested samples.\"))\n  }\n\n  return(samples)\n}\n\n# --- Test Function ---\n\n#' Run formal tests of the ARS implementation\n#' Compares samples against known distributions using statistical tests.\n#' Output format: \"TEST_NAME: PASS\" or \"TEST_NAME: FAIL\"\ntest <- function() {\n  cat(\"============================================================\\n\")\n  cat(\"     Adaptive Rejection Sampling (ARS) - Test Suite\\n\")\n  cat(\"============================================================\\n\\n\")\n\n  pass_count <- 0\n  fail_count <- 0\n\n  run_test <- function(name, expr) {\n    result <- tryCatch(expr, error = function(e) {\n      cat(paste0(\"  Error: \", e$message, \"\\n\"))\n      return(FALSE)\n    })\n    if (isTRUE(result)) {\n      cat(paste0(name, \": PASS\\n\\n\"))\n      pass_count <<- pass_count + 1\n    } else {\n      cat(paste0(name, \": FAIL\\n\\n\"))\n      fail_count <<- fail_count + 1\n    }\n  }\n\n  # ---- Input Validation Tests ----\n  cat(\"--- Input Validation Tests ---\\n\\n\")\n\n  run_test(\"test_negative_n\", {\n    cat(\"  Testing that negative n is rejected...\\n\")\n    tryCatch({ ars(dnorm, n = -5); FALSE },\n             error = function(e) grepl(\"positive integer\", e$message))\n  })\n\n  run_test(\"test_zero_n\", {\n    cat(\"  Testing that n=0 is rejected...\\n\")\n    tryCatch({ ars(dnorm, n = 0); FALSE },\n             error = function(e) grepl(\"positive integer\", e$message))\n  })\n\n  run_test(\"test_non_integer_n\", {\n    cat(\"  Testing that non-integer n is rejected...\\n\")\n    tryCatch({ ars(dnorm, n = 3.5); FALSE },\n             error = function(e) grepl(\"positive integer\", e$message))\n  })\n\n  run_test(\"test_invalid_bounds\", {\n    cat(\"  Testing that lb >= ub is rejected...\\n\")\n    tryCatch({ ars(dnorm, n = 10, lb = 5, ub = 2); FALSE },\n             error = function(e) grepl(\"strictly less\", e$message))\n  })\n\n  run_test(\"test_non_function_f\", {\n    cat(\"  Testing that non-function f is rejected...\\n\")\n    tryCatch({ ars(\"not_a_function\", n = 10); FALSE },\n             error = function(e) grepl(\"must be a function\", e$message))\n  })\n\n  # ---- Log-Concavity Tests ----\n  cat(\"--- Log-Concavity Tests ---\\n\\n\")\n\n  run_test(\"test_bimodal_rejection\", {\n    cat(\"  Testing rejection of bimodal (non-log-concave) density...\\n\")\n    bimodal <- function(x) 0.5 * dnorm(x, -3, 0.5) + 0.5 * dnorm(x, 3, 0.5)\n    tryCatch({ ars(bimodal, n = 1000, lb = -10, ub = 10); FALSE },\n             error = function(e) grepl(\"[Ll]og-concav\", e$message))\n  })\n\n  run_test(\"test_cauchy_rejection\", {\n    cat(\"  Testing rejection of Cauchy (non-log-concave) density...\\n\")\n    tryCatch({ ars(dcauchy, n = 1000, lb = -100, ub = 100); FALSE },\n             error = function(e) grepl(\"[Ll]og-concav\", e$message))\n  })\n\n  # ---- Module-Level Tests ----\n  cat(\"--- Module-Level Tests ---\\n\\n\")\n\n  run_test(\"test_eval_log_density\", {\n    cat(\"  Testing eval_log_density with dnorm...\\n\")\n    result <- eval_log_density(dnorm, 0)\n    expected <- log(dnorm(0))\n    cat(paste0(\"  log(dnorm(0)) = \", round(result, 6),\n               \" (expected \", round(expected, 6), \")\\n\"))\n    abs(result - expected) < 1e-10\n  })\n\n  run_test(\"test_check_log_concavity_valid\", {\n    cat(\"  Testing log-concavity check on concave data...\\n\")\n    x <- c(-2, -1, 0, 1, 2)\n    h <- -x^2\n    tryCatch({ check_log_concavity(x, h); TRUE }, error = function(e) FALSE)\n  })\n\n  run_test(\"test_check_log_concavity_invalid\", {\n    cat(\"  Testing log-concavity check on convex data...\\n\")\n    x <- c(-2, -1, 0, 1, 2)\n    h <- x^2  # convex, not concave\n    tryCatch({ check_log_concavity(x, h); FALSE },\n             error = function(e) grepl(\"[Ll]og-concav\", e$message))\n  })\n\n  run_test(\"test_upper_hull_above_density\", {\n    cat(\"  Testing that upper hull >= log-density at test points...\\n\")\n    x_abs <- c(-3, -1, 0, 1, 3)\n    h_abs <- eval_log_density(dnorm, x_abs)\n    hull <- compute_upper_hull(x_abs, h_abs, -Inf, Inf)\n    test_pts <- seq(-2.5, 2.5, by = 0.1)\n    h_test <- eval_log_density(dnorm, test_pts)\n    u_test <- eval_upper_hull(test_pts, hull)\n    all_above <- all(u_test >= h_test - 1e-6)\n    cat(paste0(\"  Upper hull >= log-density at all \", length(test_pts),\n               \" test points: \", all_above, \"\\n\"))\n    all_above\n  })\n\n  run_test(\"test_lower_hull_below_density\", {\n    cat(\"  Testing that lower hull <= log-density at test points...\\n\")\n    x_abs <- c(-3, -1, 0, 1, 3)\n    h_abs <- eval_log_density(dnorm, x_abs)\n    test_pts <- seq(-2.5, 2.5, by = 0.1)\n    h_test <- eval_log_density(dnorm, test_pts)\n    l_test <- sapply(test_pts, function(x) eval_lower_hull(x, x_abs, h_abs))\n    all_below <- all(l_test <= h_test + 1e-6)\n    cat(paste0(\"  Lower hull <= log-density at all \", length(test_pts),\n               \" test points: \", all_below, \"\\n\"))\n    all_below\n  })\n\n  # ---- Sampling Tests: Standard Normal ----\n  cat(\"--- Standard Normal Distribution Tests ---\\n\\n\")\n\n  run_test(\"test_normal_unbounded\", {\n    cat(\"  Sampling 5000 points from N(0,1) on (-Inf, Inf)...\\n\")\n    set.seed(42)\n    samples <- ars(n = 5000, f = dnorm, lb = -Inf, ub = Inf)\n    m <- mean(samples); s <- sd(samples)\n    cat(paste0(\"  Sample mean: \", round(m, 4), \" (expected ~0)\\n\"))\n    cat(paste0(\"  Sample sd:   \", round(s, 4), \" (expected ~1)\\n\"))\n    writeLines(as.character(samples), \"/app/normal_samples.txt\")\n    cat(\"  Saved samples to /app/normal_samples.txt\\n\")\n    ks <- ks.test(samples, \"pnorm\")\n    cat(paste0(\"  KS test p-value: \", round(ks$p.value, 4), \"\\n\"))\n    abs(m) < 0.15 && abs(s - 1) < 0.15 && ks$p.value > 0.01\n  })\n\n  run_test(\"test_normal_bounded\", {\n    cat(\"  Sampling 2000 points from N(0,1) on [-5, 5]...\\n\")\n    set.seed(123)\n    samples <- ars(dnorm, c(-5, 5), n = 2000)\n    m <- mean(samples); s <- sd(samples)\n    cat(paste0(\"  Sample mean: \", round(m, 4), \"\\n\"))\n    cat(paste0(\"  Sample sd:   \", round(s, 4), \"\\n\"))\n    cat(paste0(\"  Range: [\", round(min(samples), 4), \", \", round(max(samples), 4), \"]\\n\"))\n    all(samples >= -5 & samples <= 5) && abs(m) < 0.2\n  })\n\n  run_test(\"test_normal_shifted\", {\n    cat(\"  Sampling 3000 points from N(5, 2^2) on (-Inf, Inf)...\\n\")\n    set.seed(200)\n    shifted_norm <- function(x) dnorm(x, mean = 5, sd = 2)\n    samples <- ars(shifted_norm, n = 3000, lb = -Inf, ub = Inf)\n    m <- mean(samples); s <- sd(samples)\n    cat(paste0(\"  Sample mean: \", round(m, 4), \" (expected ~5)\\n\"))\n    cat(paste0(\"  Sample sd:   \", round(s, 4), \" (expected ~2)\\n\"))\n    ks <- ks.test(samples, \"pnorm\", mean = 5, sd = 2)\n    cat(paste0(\"  KS test p-value: \", round(ks$p.value, 4), \"\\n\"))\n    abs(m - 5) < 0.3 && abs(s - 2) < 0.3 && ks$p.value > 0.01\n  })\n\n  # ---- Sampling Tests: Exponential ----\n  cat(\"--- Exponential Distribution Tests ---\\n\\n\")\n\n  run_test(\"test_exponential_sampling\", {\n    cat(\"  Sampling 5000 points from Exp(1) on [0.001, 100]...\\n\")\n    set.seed(99)\n    samples <- ars(dexp, c(0.001, 100), n = 5000)\n    m <- mean(samples); s <- sd(samples)\n    cat(paste0(\"  Sample mean: \", round(m, 4), \" (expected ~1)\\n\"))\n    cat(paste0(\"  Sample sd:   \", round(s, 4), \" (expected ~1)\\n\"))\n    writeLines(as.character(samples), \"/app/exponential_samples.txt\")\n    cat(\"  Saved samples to /app/exponential_samples.txt\\n\")\n    ks <- ks.test(samples, \"pexp\")\n    cat(paste0(\"  KS test p-value: \", round(ks$p.value, 4), \"\\n\"))\n    abs(m - 1) < 0.2 && ks$p.value > 0.01\n  })\n\n  # ---- Sampling Tests: Gamma ----\n  cat(\"--- Gamma Distribution Tests ---\\n\\n\")\n\n  run_test(\"test_gamma_sampling\", {\n    cat(\"  Sampling 3000 points from Gamma(3, 1) on [0.01, 30]...\\n\")\n    set.seed(77)\n    gamma_dens <- function(x) dgamma(x, shape = 3, rate = 1)\n    samples <- ars(gamma_dens, c(0.01, 30), n = 3000)\n    m <- mean(samples); s <- sd(samples)\n    cat(paste0(\"  Sample mean: \", round(m, 4), \" (expected ~3)\\n\"))\n    cat(paste0(\"  Sample sd:   \", round(s, 4), \" (expected ~1.73)\\n\"))\n    ks <- ks.test(samples, \"pgamma\", shape = 3, rate = 1)\n    cat(paste0(\"  KS test p-value: \", round(ks$p.value, 4), \"\\n\"))\n    abs(m - 3) < 0.4 && ks$p.value > 0.01\n  })\n\n  # ---- Sampling Tests: Beta ----\n  cat(\"--- Beta Distribution Tests ---\\n\\n\")\n\n  run_test(\"test_beta_sampling\", {\n    cat(\"  Sampling 3000 points from Beta(2, 5) on [0.01, 0.99]...\\n\")\n    set.seed(55)\n    beta_dens <- function(x) dbeta(x, shape1 = 2, shape2 = 5)\n    samples <- ars(beta_dens, c(0.01, 0.99), n = 3000)\n    expected_mean <- 2 / (2 + 5)\n    expected_sd <- sqrt(2 * 5 / ((2 + 5)^2 * (2 + 5 + 1)))\n    m <- mean(samples); s <- sd(samples)\n    cat(paste0(\"  Sample mean: \", round(m, 4),\n               \" (expected ~\", round(expected_mean, 4), \")\\n\"))\n    cat(paste0(\"  Sample sd:   \", round(s, 4),\n               \" (expected ~\", round(expected_sd, 4), \")\\n\"))\n    ks <- ks.test(samples, \"pbeta\", shape1 = 2, shape2 = 5)\n    cat(paste0(\"  KS test p-value: \", round(ks$p.value, 4), \"\\n\"))\n    abs(m - expected_mean) < 0.05 && ks$p.value > 0.01\n  })\n\n  # ---- Sampling Tests: Unnormalized density ----\n  cat(\"--- Unnormalized Density Test ---\\n\\n\")\n\n  run_test(\"test_unnormalized_density\", {\n    cat(\"  Sampling from unnormalized Gaussian: f(x) = exp(-x^2/2)...\\n\")\n    set.seed(33)\n    unnorm_gauss <- function(x) exp(-x^2 / 2)\n    samples <- ars(unnorm_gauss, n = 3000, lb = -Inf, ub = Inf)\n    m <- mean(samples); s <- sd(samples)\n    cat(paste0(\"  Sample mean: \", round(m, 4), \" (expected ~0)\\n\"))\n    cat(paste0(\"  Sample sd:   \", round(s, 4), \" (expected ~1)\\n\"))\n    ks <- ks.test(samples, \"pnorm\")\n    cat(paste0(\"  KS test p-value: \", round(ks$p.value, 4), \"\\n\"))\n    abs(m) < 0.15 && abs(s - 1) < 0.15 && ks$p.value > 0.01\n  })\n\n  # ---- Summary ----\n  cat(\"============================================================\\n\")\n  total <- pass_count + fail_count\n  cat(paste0(\"  TOTAL: \", total, \" tests, \",\n             pass_count, \" passed, \", fail_count, \" failed\\n\"))\n  cat(\"============================================================\\n\")\n\n  invisible(list(passed = pass_count, failed = fail_count))\n}\n\n# Run tests if this script is executed directly\nif (sys.nframe() == 0) {\n  test()\n}\n", "numLines": 690, "startLine": 1, "totalLines": 690}}
