1→###############################################################################
     2→# Adaptive Rejection Sampling (ARS)
     3→# Based on Gilks, W.R. & Wild, P. (1992)
     4→# "Adaptive Rejection Sampling for Gibbs Sampling"
     5→# Journal of the Royal Statistical Society: Series C, 41(2), 337-348.
     6→#
     7→# Derivative-free implementation using secant-based upper hull construction.
     8→###############################################################################
     9→
    10→# --- Auxiliary / Module Functions ---
    11→
    12→#' Evaluate the log of a density function safely
    13→#' @param f density function (possibly unnormalized)
    14→#' @param x point(s) at which to evaluate
    15→#' @return log(f(x)), with protection against f(x) <= 0
    16→eval_log_density <- function(f, x) {
    17→  fx <- f(x)
    18→  if (any(is.na(fx) | is.nan(fx))) {
    19→    stop("Density function returned NA/NaN.")
    20→  }
    21→  if (any(fx < 0)) {
    22→    stop("Density function returned negative value.")
    23→  }
    24→  result <- rep(-Inf, length(fx))
    25→  pos <- fx > 0
    26→  result[pos] <- log(fx[pos])
    27→  return(result)
    28→}
    29→
    30→#' Check log-concavity at three consecutive abscissae
    31→#' @param abscissae sorted vector of x values
    32→#' @param h_vals corresponding log-density values h(x)
    33→#' @return TRUE if log-concave, else stops with error
    34→check_log_concavity <- function(abscissae, h_vals) {
    35→  n <- length(abscissae)
    36→  if (n < 3) return(TRUE)
    37→  for (i in 1:(n - 2)) {
    38→    x1 <- abscissae[i];   h1 <- h_vals[i]
    39→    x2 <- abscissae[i+1]; h2 <- h_vals[i+1]
    40→    x3 <- abscissae[i+2]; h3 <- h_vals[i+2]
    41→    slope_left  <- (h2 - h1) / (x2 - x1)
    42→    slope_right <- (h3 - h2) / (x3 - x2)
    43→    if (slope_right > slope_left + 1e-10) {
    44→      stop(paste0(
    45→        "Log-concavity violated at x = ", x2,
    46→        ". Left slope = ", round(slope_left, 6),
    47→        ", Right slope = ", round(slope_right, 6),
    48→        ". The density may not be log-concave."
    49→      ))
    50→    }
    51→  }
    52→  return(TRUE)
    53→}
    54→
    55→#' Compute the upper hull for the ARS algorithm (derivative-free version)
    56→#'
    57→#' For k abscissae with log-density values, the upper hull is piecewise linear
    58→#' and lies above the log-density everywhere. It is constructed from the secant
    59→#' slopes between consecutive abscissae. By log-concavity, secant slopes
    60→#' decrease: s_1 > s_2 > ... > s_{k-1}.
    61→#'
    62→#' At each interior abscissa x_j, the upper hull has a "tent" with:
    63→#'   - left arm:  slope s_j (right secant), valid for x <= x_j
    64→#'   - right arm: slope s_{j-1} (left secant), valid for x >= x_j
    65→#'
    66→#' @param abscissae sorted x values (at least 3)
    67→#' @param h_vals corresponding h(x) = log(f(x)) values
    68→#' @param lb lower bound of domain
    69→#' @param ub upper bound of domain
    70→#' @return list with segment info for the piecewise linear upper hull
    71→compute_upper_hull <- function(abscissae, h_vals, lb, ub) {
    72→  k <- length(abscissae)
    73→  if (k < 3) stop("Need at least 3 abscissae for upper hull construction.")
    74→
    75→  # Secant slopes: s[j] = slope from x_j to x_{j+1}
    76→  s <- diff(h_vals) / diff(abscissae)
    77→
    78→  # Build the list of line segments for the upper hull.
    79→  # Each segment is defined by a point (px, ph) and slope m:
    80→  #   u(x) = ph + m * (x - px) = (ph - m*px) + m*x
    81→  #
    82→  # Segments from left to right:
    83→  # 1. x_1's left arm: slope s[1], through (x_1, h_1), for x from lb to z_1
    84→  # Then for each interior point j = 2,...,k-1:
    85→  #   left arm:  slope s[j], through (x_j, h_j)
    86→  #   right arm: slope s[j-1], through (x_j, h_j)
    87→  # Last: x_k's right arm: slope s[k-1], through (x_k, h_k), for x from z_{k-1} to ub
    88→
    89→  n_seg <- 2 * (k - 1)
    90→  seg_px <- numeric(n_seg)
    91→  seg_ph <- numeric(n_seg)
    92→  seg_m  <- numeric(n_seg)
    93→
    94→  idx <- 1
    95→  # First segment: x_1, slope s[1]
    96→  seg_px[idx] <- abscissae[1]; seg_ph[idx] <- h_vals[1]; seg_m[idx] <- s[1]
    97→  idx <- idx + 1
    98→
    99→  # Interior points
   100→  for (j in 2:(k-1)) {
   101→    # Left arm: slope s[j]
   102→    seg_px[idx] <- abscissae[j]; seg_ph[idx] <- h_vals[j]; seg_m[idx] <- s[j]
   103→    idx <- idx + 1
   104→    # Right arm: slope s[j-1]
   105→    seg_px[idx] <- abscissae[j]; seg_ph[idx] <- h_vals[j]; seg_m[idx] <- s[j-1]
   106→    idx <- idx + 1
   107→  }
   108→
   109→  # Last segment: x_k, slope s[k-1]
   110→  seg_px[idx] <- abscissae[k]; seg_ph[idx] <- h_vals[k]; seg_m[idx] <- s[k-1]
   111→
   112→  # Convert to intercept form: u(x) = intercept + slope * x
   113→  seg_int <- seg_ph - seg_m * seg_px
   114→
   115→  # Compute breakpoints between consecutive segments
   116→  bp <- numeric(n_seg - 1)
   117→  for (i in 1:(n_seg - 1)) {
   118→    if (seg_px[i] == seg_px[i+1] && seg_ph[i] == seg_ph[i+1]) {
   119→      # Same point (left/right arm of same tent): breakpoint is at that x
   120→      bp[i] <- seg_px[i]
   121→    } else {
   122→      # Intersection of two different lines
   123→      bp[i] <- (seg_int[i+1] - seg_int[i]) / (seg_m[i] - seg_m[i+1])
   124→    }
   125→  }
   126→
   127→  z <- c(lb, bp, ub)
   128→
   129→  return(list(
   130→    z = z,
   131→    seg_slopes = seg_m,
   132→    seg_intercepts = seg_int,
   133→    n_seg = n_seg
   134→  ))
   135→}
   136→
   137→#' Evaluate the upper hull at a point x
   138→#' @param x scalar or vector of points
   139→#' @param hull list from compute_upper_hull
   140→#' @return u(x), the upper hull value(s)
   141→eval_upper_hull <- function(x, hull) {
   142→  z <- hull$z
   143→  m <- hull$seg_slopes
   144→  b <- hull$seg_intercepts
   145→  n_seg <- hull$n_seg
   146→
   147→  seg_idx <- findInterval(x, z, rightmost.closed = TRUE)
   148→  seg_idx <- pmax(1, pmin(seg_idx, n_seg))
   149→
   150→  return(b[seg_idx] + m[seg_idx] * x)
   151→}
   152→
   153→#' Evaluate the lower hull (piecewise linear through abscissae) at a point x
   154→#' @param x scalar point
   155→#' @param abscissae sorted x values
   156→#' @param h_vals corresponding h(x) values
   157→#' @return l(x), the lower hull value. -Inf if outside the range of abscissae.
   158→eval_lower_hull <- function(x, abscissae, h_vals) {
   159→  k <- length(abscissae)
   160→  if (x <= abscissae[1] || x >= abscissae[k]) {
   161→    return(-Inf)
   162→  }
   163→  j <- findInterval(x, abscissae)
   164→  j <- pmax(1, pmin(j, k - 1))
   165→  t <- (x - abscissae[j]) / (abscissae[j+1] - abscissae[j])
   166→  return(h_vals[j] * (1 - t) + h_vals[j+1] * t)
   167→}
   168→
   169→#' Sample from the upper hull (piecewise-exponential distribution)
   170→#' @param n number of samples to draw
   171→#' @param hull list from compute_upper_hull
   172→#' @return vector of n samples
   173→sample_upper_hull <- function(n, hull) {
   174→  z <- hull$z
   175→  m <- hull$seg_slopes
   176→  b <- hull$seg_intercepts
   177→  n_seg <- hull$n_seg
   178→
   179→  # Compute area under each segment: integral of exp(b + m*x) from z[j] to z[j+1]
   180→  areas <- numeric(n_seg)
   181→  for (j in 1:n_seg) {
   182→    lo <- z[j]
   183→    hi <- z[j + 1]
   184→
   185→    # Handle infinite bounds: for the integral to converge, slope must be favorable
   186→    if (!is.finite(lo)) {
   187→      # lo = -Inf. Need m[j] > 0 for convergence
   188→      if (m[j] <= 0) {
   189→        areas[j] <- 0
   190→        next
   191→      }
   192→      # Integral of exp(b + m*x) from -Inf to hi = exp(b + m*hi) / m
   193→      areas[j] <- exp(b[j] + m[j] * hi) / m[j]
   194→      next
   195→    }
   196→    if (!is.finite(hi)) {
   197→      # hi = +Inf. Need m[j] < 0 for convergence
   198→      if (m[j] >= 0) {
   199→        areas[j] <- 0
   200→        next
   201→      }
   202→      # Integral of exp(b + m*x) from lo to Inf = -exp(b + m*lo) / m
   203→      areas[j] <- -exp(b[j] + m[j] * lo) / m[j]
   204→      next
   205→    }
   206→
   207→    if (abs(m[j]) < 1e-15) {
   208→      areas[j] <- exp(b[j]) * (hi - lo)
   209→    } else {
   210→      # Integral = (exp(b + m*hi) - exp(b + m*lo)) / m
   211→      # For numerical stability, factor out the larger value
   212→      log_hi <- b[j] + m[j] * hi
   213→      log_lo <- b[j] + m[j] * lo
   214→      log_max <- max(log_hi, log_lo)
   215→      areas[j] <- exp(log_max) * (exp(log_hi - log_max) - exp(log_lo - log_max)) / m[j]
   216→    }
   217→
   218→    if (is.na(areas[j]) || !is.finite(areas[j]) || areas[j] < 0) {
   219→      areas[j] <- 0
   220→    }
   221→  }
   222→
   223→  total_area <- sum(areas)
   224→  if (total_area <= 0 || !is.finite(total_area)) {
   225→    stop("Upper hull has zero or infinite area. Check domain bounds and initial abscissae.")
   226→  }
   227→  probs <- areas / total_area
   228→
   229→  # Sample segments according to their areas
   230→  seg_choices <- sample.int(n_seg, size = n, replace = TRUE, prob = probs)
   231→
   232→  # For each chosen segment, sample from the truncated exponential via inverse CDF
   233→  samples <- numeric(n)
   234→  for (i in 1:n) {
   235→    j <- seg_choices[i]
   236→    lo <- z[j]
   237→    hi <- z[j + 1]
   238→    u <- runif(1)
   239→
   240→    if (abs(m[j]) < 1e-15) {
   241→      # Uniform on [lo, hi]
   242→      if (!is.finite(lo)) lo <- hi - 20
   243→      if (!is.finite(hi)) hi <- lo + 20
   244→      samples[i] <- lo + u * (hi - lo)
   245→    } else if (!is.finite(lo)) {
   246→      # lo = -Inf, m > 0: CDF(x) = exp(m*(x-hi)) for x <= hi
   247→      # Inverse: x = hi + log(u) / m
   248→      samples[i] <- hi + log(u) / m[j]
   249→    } else if (!is.finite(hi)) {
   250→      # hi = +Inf, m < 0: CDF(x) = 1 - exp(m*(x-lo)) for x >= lo
   251→      # Inverse: x = lo + log(1 - u) / m  ... but log(1-u) ~ log(u) in distribution
   252→      samples[i] <- lo + log(u) / m[j]  # since m < 0, this gives x > lo
   253→    } else {
   254→      # Both finite: inverse CDF of truncated exponential
   255→      log_lo <- m[j] * lo
   256→      log_hi <- m[j] * hi
   257→      # CDF: F(x) = (exp(m*x) - exp(m*lo)) / (exp(m*hi) - exp(m*lo))
   258→      # Inverse: x = log(exp(m*lo) + u*(exp(m*hi) - exp(m*lo))) / m
   259→      log_max <- max(log_lo, log_hi)
   260→      exp_lo <- exp(log_lo - log_max)
   261→      exp_hi <- exp(log_hi - log_max)
   262→      samples[i] <- (log_max + log(exp_lo + u * (exp_hi - exp_lo))) / m[j]
   263→    }
   264→  }
   265→  return(samples)
   266→}
   267→
   268→#' Choose initial abscissae for the ARS algorithm
   269→#' Ensures at least 3 points with positive density, and that
   270→#' boundary slopes are compatible with infinite domains.
   271→#' @param lb lower bound
   272→#' @param ub upper bound
   273→#' @param f density function
   274→#' @param n_init number of initial points (default 5, minimum 3)
   275→#' @return vector of initial abscissae
   276→choose_initial_abscissae <- function(lb, ub, f, n_init = 20) {
   277→  n_init <- max(n_init, 5)
   278→
   279→  # Generate candidate points
   280→  if (is.finite(lb) && is.finite(ub)) {
   281→    margin <- (ub - lb) * 0.02
   282→    pts <- seq(lb + margin, ub - margin, length.out = n_init)
   283→  } else if (is.finite(lb) && !is.finite(ub)) {
   284→    pts <- lb + c(0.1, 0.5, 1, 2, 3, 5, 8, 12, 18, 25, 35, 50,
   285→                  0.2, 0.8, 1.5, 4, 7, 10, 15, 20)[1:n_init]
   286→  } else if (!is.finite(lb) && is.finite(ub)) {
   287→    pts <- ub - c(0.1, 0.5, 1, 2, 3, 5, 8, 12, 18, 25, 35, 50,
   288→                  0.2, 0.8, 1.5, 4, 7, 10, 15, 20)[1:n_init]
   289→  } else {
   290→    # Both infinite: center around 0
   291→    pts <- c(seq(-4, 4, length.out = n_init))
   292→  }
   293→
   294→  pts <- sort(unique(pts))
   295→
   296→  # Keep only points with positive density
   297→  vals <- f(pts)
   298→  good <- which(vals > 0 & !is.na(vals) & is.finite(vals))
   299→  if (length(good) < 3) {
   300→    stop("Cannot find at least 3 initial abscissae with positive density. Check domain bounds.")
   301→  }
   302→  pts <- pts[good]
   303→
   304→  # Compute log-density
   305→  h <- log(vals[good])
   306→
   307→  # For infinite left bound: need first secant slope > 0
   308→  # For infinite right bound: need last secant slope < 0
   309→  # Trim from each end until this is satisfied
   310→  if (!is.finite(lb)) {
   311→    while (length(pts) > 3) {
   312→      s1 <- (h[2] - h[1]) / (pts[2] - pts[1])
   313→      if (s1 > 0) break
   314→      pts <- pts[-1]; h <- h[-1]
   315→    }
   316→  }
   317→  if (!is.finite(ub)) {
   318→    while (length(pts) > 3) {
   319→      k <- length(pts)
   320→      sk <- (h[k] - h[k-1]) / (pts[k] - pts[k-1])
   321→      if (sk < 0) break
   322→      pts <- pts[-k]; h <- h[-k]
   323→    }
   324→  }
   325→
   326→  if (length(pts) < 3) {
   327→    stop("Cannot find suitable initial abscissae. The density mode may be outside the initial search range.")
   328→  }
   329→
   330→  return(pts)
   331→}
   332→
   333→#' Validate inputs to the ars function
   334→#' @param f density function
   335→#' @param n number of samples
   336→#' @param lb lower bound
   337→#' @param ub upper bound
   338→validate_inputs <- function(f, n, lb, ub) {
   339→  if (!is.function(f)) {
   340→    stop("'f' must be a function.")
   341→  }
   342→  if (!is.numeric(n) || length(n) != 1 || n < 1 || n != as.integer(n)) {
   343→    stop("'n' must be a positive integer.")
   344→  }
   345→  if (!is.numeric(lb) || length(lb) != 1) {
   346→    stop("'lb' must be a single numeric value.")
   347→  }
   348→  if (!is.numeric(ub) || length(ub) != 1) {
   349→    stop("'ub' must be a single numeric value.")
   350→  }
   351→  if (lb >= ub) {
   352→    stop("Lower bound must be strictly less than upper bound.")
   353→  }
   354→}
   355→
   356→# --- Main ARS Function ---
   357→
   358→#' Adaptive Rejection Sampling
   359→#'
   360→#' @param f density function (possibly unnormalized), must be log-concave.
   361→#'          Should accept a vector and return a vector of density values.
   362→#' @param support optional 2-element vector c(lb, ub) for the domain
   363→#' @param n number of samples to generate (default 100)
   364→#' @param lb lower bound of the domain (default -Inf)
   365→#' @param ub upper bound of the domain (default Inf)
   366→#' @param ... additional arguments (currently unused)
   367→#' @return vector of n samples from the target distribution
   368→ars <- function(f, support = NULL, n = 100, lb = -Inf, ub = Inf, ...) {
   369→  # Handle the support vector argument (alternative call pattern)
   370→  if (!is.null(support) && is.numeric(support) && length(support) == 2) {
   371→    lb <- support[1]
   372→    ub <- support[2]
   373→  }
   374→
   375→  # Validate inputs
   376→  validate_inputs(f, n, lb, ub)
   377→
   378→  # Choose initial abscissae (at least 3)
   379→  abscissae <- choose_initial_abscissae(lb, ub, f)
   380→  h_vals <- eval_log_density(f, abscissae)
   381→
   382→  # Check initial log-concavity
   383→  check_log_concavity(abscissae, h_vals)
   384→
   385→  # Main sampling loop
   386→  samples <- numeric(n)
   387→  count <- 0
   388→  max_iter <- n * 200  # safety limit
   389→  iter <- 0
   390→
   391→  while (count < n && iter < max_iter) {
   392→    iter <- iter + 1
   393→
   394→    # Build upper hull
   395→    hull <- compute_upper_hull(abscissae, h_vals, lb, ub)
   396→
   397→    # Sample a candidate from the upper hull
   398→    x_star <- sample_upper_hull(1, hull)
   399→
   400→    # Squeeze and rejection test
   401→    u <- runif(1)
   402→    u_val <- eval_upper_hull(x_star, hull)
   403→    l_val <- eval_lower_hull(x_star, abscissae, h_vals)
   404→
   405→    if (u <= exp(l_val - u_val)) {
   406→      # Accept by squeeze test (no density evaluation needed)
   407→      count <- count + 1
   408→      samples[count] <- x_star
   409→    } else {
   410→      # Need to evaluate the true log-density
   411→      h_star <- eval_log_density(f, x_star)
   412→
   413→      if (u <= exp(h_star - u_val)) {
   414→        # Accept by rejection test
   415→        count <- count + 1
   416→        samples[count] <- x_star
   417→      }
   418→
   419→      # Update abscissae with new point (whether accepted or not)
   420→      insert_pos <- findInterval(x_star, abscissae) + 1
   421→      abscissae <- append(abscissae, x_star, after = insert_pos - 1)
   422→      h_vals <- append(h_vals, h_star, after = insert_pos - 1)
   423→
   424→      # Re-check log-concavity with new point
   425→      check_log_concavity(abscissae, h_vals)
   426→    }
   427→  }
   428→
   429→  if (count < n) {
   430→    warning(paste("Only generated", count, "out of", n, "requested samples."))
   431→  }
   432→
   433→  return(samples)
   434→}
   435→
   436→# --- Test Function ---
   437→
   438→#' Run formal tests of the ARS implementation
   439→#' Compares samples against known distributions using statistical tests.
   440→#' Output format: "TEST_NAME: PASS" or "TEST_NAME: FAIL"
   441→test <- function() {
   442→  cat("============================================================\n")
   443→  cat("     Adaptive Rejection Sampling (ARS) - Test Suite\n")
   444→  cat("============================================================\n\n")
   445→
   446→  pass_count <- 0
   447→  fail_count <- 0
   448→
   449→  run_test <- function(name, expr) {
   450→    result <- tryCatch(expr, error = function(e) {
   451→      cat(paste0("  Error: ", e$message, "\n"))
   452→      return(FALSE)
   453→    })
   454→    if (isTRUE(result)) {
   455→      cat(paste0(name, ": PASS\n\n"))
   456→      pass_count <<- pass_count + 1
   457→    } else {
   458→      cat(paste0(name, ": FAIL\n\n"))
   459→      fail_count <<- fail_count + 1
   460→    }
   461→  }
   462→
   463→  # ---- Input Validation Tests ----
   464→  cat("--- Input Validation Tests ---\n\n")
   465→
   466→  run_test("test_negative_n", {
   467→    cat("  Testing that negative n is rejected...\n")
   468→    tryCatch({ ars(dnorm, n = -5); FALSE },
   469→             error = function(e) grepl("positive integer", e$message))
   470→  })
   471→
   472→  run_test("test_zero_n", {
   473→    cat("  Testing that n=0 is rejected...\n")
   474→    tryCatch({ ars(dnorm, n = 0); FALSE },
   475→             error = function(e) grepl("positive integer", e$message))
   476→  })
   477→
   478→  run_test("test_non_integer_n", {
   479→    cat("  Testing that non-integer n is rejected...\n")
   480→    tryCatch({ ars(dnorm, n = 3.5); FALSE },
   481→             error = function(e) grepl("positive integer", e$message))
   482→  })
   483→
   484→  run_test("test_invalid_bounds", {
   485→    cat("  Testing that lb >= ub is rejected...\n")
   486→    tryCatch({ ars(dnorm, n = 10, lb = 5, ub = 2); FALSE },
   487→             error = function(e) grepl("strictly less", e$message))
   488→  })
   489→
   490→  run_test("test_non_function_f", {
   491→    cat("  Testing that non-function f is rejected...\n")
   492→    tryCatch({ ars("not_a_function", n = 10); FALSE },
   493→             error = function(e) grepl("must be a function", e$message))
   494→  })
   495→
   496→  # ---- Log-Concavity Tests ----
   497→  cat("--- Log-Concavity Tests ---\n\n")
   498→
   499→  run_test("test_bimodal_rejection", {
   500→    cat("  Testing rejection of bimodal (non-log-concave) density...\n")
   501→    bimodal <- function(x) 0.5 * dnorm(x, -3, 0.5) + 0.5 * dnorm(x, 3, 0.5)
   502→    tryCatch({ ars(bimodal, n = 1000, lb = -10, ub = 10); FALSE },
   503→             error = function(e) grepl("[Ll]og-concav", e$message))
   504→  })
   505→
   506→  run_test("test_cauchy_rejection", {
   507→    cat("  Testing rejection of Cauchy (non-log-concave) density...\n")
   508→    tryCatch({ ars(dcauchy, n = 1000, lb = -100, ub = 100); FALSE },
   509→             error = function(e) grepl("[Ll]og-concav", e$message))
   510→  })
   511→
   512→  # ---- Module-Level Tests ----
   513→  cat("--- Module-Level Tests ---\n\n")
   514→
   515→  run_test("test_eval_log_density", {
   516→    cat("  Testing eval_log_density with dnorm...\n")
   517→    result <- eval_log_density(dnorm, 0)
   518→    expected <- log(dnorm(0))
   519→    cat(paste0("  log(dnorm(0)) = ", round(result, 6),
   520→               " (expected ", round(expected, 6), ")\n"))
   521→    abs(result - expected) < 1e-10
   522→  })
   523→
   524→  run_test("test_check_log_concavity_valid", {
   525→    cat("  Testing log-concavity check on concave data...\n")
   526→    x <- c(-2, -1, 0, 1, 2)
   527→    h <- -x^2
   528→    tryCatch({ check_log_concavity(x, h); TRUE }, error = function(e) FALSE)
   529→  })
   530→
   531→  run_test("test_check_log_concavity_invalid", {
   532→    cat("  Testing log-concavity check on convex data...\n")
   533→    x <- c(-2, -1, 0, 1, 2)
   534→    h <- x^2  # convex, not concave
   535→    tryCatch({ check_log_concavity(x, h); FALSE },
   536→             error = function(e) grepl("[Ll]og-concav", e$message))
   537→  })
   538→
   539→  run_test("test_upper_hull_above_density", {
   540→    cat("  Testing that upper hull >= log-density at test points...\n")
   541→    x_abs <- c(-3, -1, 0, 1, 3)
   542→    h_abs <- eval_log_density(dnorm, x_abs)
   543→    hull <- compute_upper_hull(x_abs, h_abs, -Inf, Inf)
   544→    test_pts <- seq(-2.5, 2.5, by = 0.1)
   545→    h_test <- eval_log_density(dnorm, test_pts)
   546→    u_test <- eval_upper_hull(test_pts, hull)
   547→    all_above <- all(u_test >= h_test - 1e-6)
   548→    cat(paste0("  Upper hull >= log-density at all ", length(test_pts),
   549→               " test points: ", all_above, "\n"))
   550→    all_above
   551→  })
   552→
   553→  run_test("test_lower_hull_below_density", {
   554→    cat("  Testing that lower hull <= log-density at test points...\n")
   555→    x_abs <- c(-3, -1, 0, 1, 3)
   556→    h_abs <- eval_log_density(dnorm, x_abs)
   557→    test_pts <- seq(-2.5, 2.5, by = 0.1)
   558→    h_test <- eval_log_density(dnorm, test_pts)
   559→    l_test <- sapply(test_pts, function(x) eval_lower_hull(x, x_abs, h_abs))
   560→    all_below <- all(l_test <= h_test + 1e-6)
   561→    cat(paste0("  Lower hull <= log-density at all ", length(test_pts),
   562→               " test points: ", all_below, "\n"))
   563→    all_below
   564→  })
   565→
   566→  # ---- Sampling Tests: Standard Normal ----
   567→  cat("--- Standard Normal Distribution Tests ---\n\n")
   568→
   569→  run_test("test_normal_unbounded", {
   570→    cat("  Sampling 5000 points from N(0,1) on (-Inf, Inf)...\n")
   571→    set.seed(42)
   572→    samples <- ars(n = 5000, f = dnorm, lb = -Inf, ub = Inf)
   573→    m <- mean(samples); s <- sd(samples)
   574→    cat(paste0("  Sample mean: ", round(m, 4), " (expected ~0)\n"))
   575→    cat(paste0("  Sample sd:   ", round(s, 4), " (expected ~1)\n"))
   576→    writeLines(as.character(samples), "/app/normal_samples.txt")
   577→    cat("  Saved samples to /app/normal_samples.txt\n")
   578→    ks <- ks.test(samples, "pnorm")
   579→    cat(paste0("  KS test p-value: ", round(ks$p.value, 4), "\n"))
   580→    abs(m) < 0.15 && abs(s - 1) < 0.15 && ks$p.value > 0.01
   581→  })
   582→
   583→  run_test("test_normal_bounded", {
   584→    cat("  Sampling 2000 points from N(0,1) on [-5, 5]...\n")
   585→    set.seed(123)
   586→    samples <- ars(dnorm, c(-5, 5), n = 2000)
   587→    m <- mean(samples); s <- sd(samples)
   588→    cat(paste0("  Sample mean: ", round(m, 4), "\n"))
   589→    cat(paste0("  Sample sd:   ", round(s, 4), "\n"))
   590→    cat(paste0("  Range: [", round(min(samples), 4), ", ", round(max(samples), 4), "]\n"))
   591→    all(samples >= -5 & samples <= 5) && abs(m) < 0.2
   592→  })
   593→
   594→  run_test("test_normal_shifted", {
   595→    cat("  Sampling 3000 points from N(5, 2^2) on (-Inf, Inf)...\n")
   596→    set.seed(200)
   597→    shifted_norm <- function(x) dnorm(x, mean = 5, sd = 2)
   598→    samples <- ars(shifted_norm, n = 3000, lb = -Inf, ub = Inf)
   599→    m <- mean(samples); s <- sd(samples)
   600→    cat(paste0("  Sample mean: ", round(m, 4), " (expected ~5)\n"))
   601→    cat(paste0("  Sample sd:   ", round(s, 4), " (expected ~2)\n"))
   602→    ks <- ks.test(samples, "pnorm", mean = 5, sd = 2)
   603→    cat(paste0("  KS test p-value: ", round(ks$p.value, 4), "\n"))
   604→    abs(m - 5) < 0.3 && abs(s - 2) < 0.3 && ks$p.value > 0.01
   605→  })
   606→
   607→  # ---- Sampling Tests: Exponential ----
   608→  cat("--- Exponential Distribution Tests ---\n\n")
   609→
   610→  run_test("test_exponential_sampling", {
   611→    cat("  Sampling 5000 points from Exp(1) on [0.001, 100]...\n")
   612→    set.seed(99)
   613→    samples <- ars(dexp, c(0.001, 100), n = 5000)
   614→    m <- mean(samples); s <- sd(samples)
   615→    cat(paste0("  Sample mean: ", round(m, 4), " (expected ~1)\n"))
   616→    cat(paste0("  Sample sd:   ", round(s, 4), " (expected ~1)\n"))
   617→    writeLines(as.character(samples), "/app/exponential_samples.txt")
   618→    cat("  Saved samples to /app/exponential_samples.txt\n")
   619→    ks <- ks.test(samples, "pexp")
   620→    cat(paste0("  KS test p-value: ", round(ks$p.value, 4), "\n"))
   621→    abs(m - 1) < 0.2 && ks$p.value > 0.01
   622→  })
   623→
   624→  # ---- Sampling Tests: Gamma ----
   625→  cat("--- Gamma Distribution Tests ---\n\n")
   626→
   627→  run_test("test_gamma_sampling", {
   628→    cat("  Sampling 3000 points from Gamma(3, 1) on [0.01, 30]...\n")
   629→    set.seed(77)
   630→    gamma_dens <- function(x) dgamma(x, shape = 3, rate = 1)
   631→    samples <- ars(gamma_dens, c(0.01, 30), n = 3000)
   632→    m <- mean(samples); s <- sd(samples)
   633→    cat(paste0("  Sample mean: ", round(m, 4), " (expected ~3)\n"))

   635→    ks <- ks.test(samples, "pgamma", shape = 3, rate = 1)

   645→    set.seed(55)

   647→    samples <- ars(beta_dens, c(0.01, 0.99), n = 3000)
   648→    expected_mean <- 2 / (2 + 5)

   650→    m <- mean(samples); s <- sd(samples)

   653→    cat(paste0("  Sample sd:   ", round(s, 4),

   655→    ks <- ks.test(samples, "pbeta", shape1 = 2, shape2 = 5)
   656→    cat(paste0("  KS test p-value: ", round(ks$p.value, 4), "\n"))

   658→  })

   660→  # ---- Sampling Tests: Unnormalized density ----

   667→    samples <- ars(unnorm_gauss, n = 3000, lb = -Inf, ub = Inf)
   668→    m <- mean(samples); s <- sd(samples)

   680→             pass_count, " passed, ", fail_count, " failed\n"))

1→###############################################################################
     2→# Adaptive Rejection Sampling (ARS)
     3→# Based on Gilks, W.R. & Wild, P. (1992)
     4→# "Adaptive Rejection Sampling for Gibbs Sampling"
     5→# Journal of the Royal Statistical Society: Series C, 41(2), 337-348.
     6→#
     7→# Derivative-free implementation using secant-based upper hull construction.
     8→###############################################################################
     9→
    10→# --- Auxiliary / Module Functions ---
    11→
    12→#' Evaluate the log of a density function safely
    13→#' @param f density function (possibly unnormalized)
    14→#' @param x point(s) at which to evaluate
    15→#' @return log(f(x)), with protection against f(x) <= 0
    16→eval_log_density <- function(f, x) {
    17→  fx <- f(x)
    18→  if (any(is.na(fx) | is.nan(fx))) {
    19→    stop("Density function returned NA/NaN.")
    20→  }
    21→  if (any(fx < 0)) {
    22→    stop("Density function returned negative value.")
    23→  }
    24→  result <- rep(-Inf, length(fx))
    25→  pos <- fx > 0
    26→  result[pos] <- log(fx[pos])
    27→  return(result)
    28→}
    29→
    30→#' Check log-concavity at three consecutive abscissae
    31→#' @param abscissae sorted vector of x values
    32→#' @param h_vals corresponding log-density values h(x)
    33→#' @return TRUE if log-concave, else stops with error
    34→check_log_concavity <- function(abscissae, h_vals) {
    35→  n <- length(abscissae)
    36→  if (n < 3) return(TRUE)
    37→  for (i in 1:(n - 2)) {
    38→    x1 <- abscissae[i];   h1 <- h_vals[i]
    39→    x2 <- abscissae[i+1]; h2 <- h_vals[i+1]
    40→    x3 <- abscissae[i+2]; h3 <- h_vals[i+2]
    41→    slope_left  <- (h2 - h1) / (x2 - x1)
    42→    slope_right <- (h3 - h2) / (x3 - x2)
    43→    if (slope_right > slope_left + 1e-10) {
    44→      stop(paste0(
    45→        "Log-concavity violated at x = ", x2,
    46→        ". Left slope = ", round(slope_left, 6),
    47→        ", Right slope = ", round(slope_right, 6),
    48→        ". The density may not be log-concave."
    49→      ))
    50→    }
    51→  }
    52→  return(TRUE)
    53→}
    54→
    55→#' Compute the upper hull for the ARS algorithm (derivative-free version)
    56→#'
    57→#' For k abscissae with log-density values, the upper hull is piecewise linear
    58→#' and lies above the log-density everywhere. It is constructed from the secant
    59→#' slopes between consecutive abscissae. By log-concavity, secant slopes
    60→#' decrease: s_1 > s_2 > ... > s_{k-1}.
    61→#'
    62→#' At each interior abscissa x_j, the upper hull has a "tent" with:
    63→#'   - left arm:  slope s_j (right secant), valid for x <= x_j
    64→#'   - right arm: slope s_{j-1} (left secant), valid for x >= x_j
    65→#'
    66→#' @param abscissae sorted x values (at least 3)
    67→#' @param h_vals corresponding h(x) = log(f(x)) values
    68→#' @param lb lower bound of domain
    69→#' @param ub upper bound of domain
    70→#' @return list with segment info for the piecewise linear upper hull
    71→compute_upper_hull <- function(abscissae, h_vals, lb, ub) {
    72→  k <- length(abscissae)
    73→  if (k < 3) stop("Need at least 3 abscissae for upper hull construction.")
    74→
    75→  # Secant slopes: s[j] = slope from x_j to x_{j+1}
    76→  s <- diff(h_vals) / diff(abscissae)
    77→
    78→  # Build the list of line segments for the upper hull.
    79→  # Each segment is defined by a point (px, ph) and slope m:
    80→  #   u(x) = ph + m * (x - px) = (ph - m*px) + m*x
    81→  #
    82→  # Segments from left to right:
    83→  # 1. x_1's left arm: slope s[1], through (x_1, h_1), for x from lb to z_1
    84→  # Then for each interior point j = 2,...,k-1:
    85→  #   left arm:  slope s[j], through (x_j, h_j)
    86→  #   right arm: slope s[j-1], through (x_j, h_j)
    87→  # Last: x_k's right arm: slope s[k-1], through (x_k, h_k), for x from z_{k-1} to ub
    88→
    89→  n_seg <- 2 * (k - 1)
    90→  seg_px <- numeric(n_seg)
    91→  seg_ph <- numeric(n_seg)
    92→  seg_m  <- numeric(n_seg)
    93→
    94→  idx <- 1
    95→  # First segment: x_1, slope s[1]
    96→  seg_px[idx] <- abscissae[1]; seg_ph[idx] <- h_vals[1]; seg_m[idx] <- s[1]
    97→  idx <- idx + 1
    98→
    99→  # Interior points
   100→  for (j in 2:(k-1)) {
   101→    # Left arm: slope s[j]
   102→    seg_px[idx] <- abscissae[j]; seg_ph[idx] <- h_vals[j]; seg_m[idx] <- s[j]
   103→    idx <- idx + 1
   104→    # Right arm: slope s[j-1]
   105→    seg_px[idx] <- abscissae[j]; seg_ph[idx] <- h_vals[j]; seg_m[idx] <- s[j-1]
   106→    idx <- idx + 1
   107→  }
   108→
   109→  # Last segment: x_k, slope s[k-1]
   110→  seg_px[idx] <- abscissae[k]; seg_ph[idx] <- h_vals[k]; seg_m[idx] <- s[k-1]
   111→
   112→  # Convert to intercept form: u(x) = intercept + slope * x
   113→  seg_int <- seg_ph - seg_m * seg_px
   114→
   115→  # Compute breakpoints between consecutive segments
   116→  bp <- numeric(n_seg - 1)
   117→  for (i in 1:(n_seg - 1)) {
   118→    if (seg_px[i] == seg_px[i+1] && seg_ph[i] == seg_ph[i+1]) {
   119→      # Same point (left/right arm of same tent): breakpoint is at that x
   120→      bp[i] <- seg_px[i]
   121→    } else {
   122→      # Intersection of two different lines
   123→      bp[i] <- (seg_int[i+1] - seg_int[i]) / (seg_m[i] - seg_m[i+1])
   124→    }
   125→  }
   126→
   127→  z <- c(lb, bp, ub)
   128→
   129→  return(list(
   130→    z = z,
   131→    seg_slopes = seg_m,
   132→    seg_intercepts = seg_int,
   133→    n_seg = n_seg
   134→  ))
   135→}
   136→
   137→#' Evaluate the upper hull at a point x
   138→#' @param x scalar or vector of points
   139→#' @param hull list from compute_upper_hull
   140→#' @return u(x), the upper hull value(s)
   141→eval_upper_hull <- function(x, hull) {
   142→  z <- hull$z
   143→  m <- hull$seg_slopes
   144→  b <- hull$seg_intercepts
   145→  n_seg <- hull$n_seg
   146→
   147→  seg_idx <- findInterval(x, z, rightmost.closed = TRUE)
   148→  seg_idx <- pmax(1, pmin(seg_idx, n_seg))
   149→
   150→  return(b[seg_idx] + m[seg_idx] * x)
   151→}
   152→
   153→#' Evaluate the lower hull (piecewise linear through abscissae) at a point x
   154→#' @param x scalar point
   155→#' @param abscissae sorted x values
   156→#' @param h_vals corresponding h(x) values
   157→#' @return l(x), the lower hull value. -Inf if outside the range of abscissae.
   158→eval_lower_hull <- function(x, abscissae, h_vals) {
   159→  k <- length(abscissae)
   160→  if (x <= abscissae[1] || x >= abscissae[k]) {
   161→    return(-Inf)
   162→  }
   163→  j <- findInterval(x, abscissae)
   164→  j <- pmax(1, pmin(j, k - 1))
   165→  t <- (x - abscissae[j]) / (abscissae[j+1] - abscissae[j])
   166→  return(h_vals[j] * (1 - t) + h_vals[j+1] * t)
   167→}
   168→
   169→#' Sample from the upper hull (piecewise-exponential distribution)
   170→#' @param n number of samples to draw
   171→#' @param hull list from compute_upper_hull
   172→#' @return vector of n samples
   173→sample_upper_hull <- function(n, hull) {
   174→  z <- hull$z
   175→  m <- hull$seg_slopes
   176→  b <- hull$seg_intercepts
   177→  n_seg <- hull$n_seg
   178→
   179→  # Compute area under each segment: integral of exp(b + m*x) from z[j] to z[j+1]
   180→  areas <- numeric(n_seg)
   181→  for (j in 1:n_seg) {
   182→    lo <- z[j]
   183→    hi <- z[j + 1]
   184→
   185→    # Handle infinite bounds: for the integral to converge, slope must be favorable
   186→    if (!is.finite(lo)) {
   187→      # lo = -Inf. Need m[j] > 0 for convergence
   188→      if (m[j] <= 0) {
   189→        areas[j] <- 0
   190→        next
   191→      }
   192→      # Integral of exp(b + m*x) from -Inf to hi = exp(b + m*hi) / m
   193→      areas[j] <- exp(b[j] + m[j] * hi) / m[j]
   194→      next
   195→    }
   196→    if (!is.finite(hi)) {
   197→      # hi = +Inf. Need m[j] < 0 for convergence
   198→      if (m[j] >= 0) {
   199→        areas[j] <- 0
   200→        next
   201→      }
   202→      # Integral of exp(b + m*x) from lo to Inf = -exp(b + m*lo) / m
   203→      areas[j] <- -exp(b[j] + m[j] * lo) / m[j]
   204→      next
   205→    }
   206→
   207→    if (abs(m[j]) < 1e-15) {
   208→      areas[j] <- exp(b[j]) * (hi - lo)
   209→    } else {
   210→      # Integral = (exp(b + m*hi) - exp(b + m*lo)) / m
   211→      # For numerical stability, factor out the larger value
   212→      log_hi <- b[j] + m[j] * hi
   213→      log_lo <- b[j] + m[j] * lo
   214→      log_max <- max(log_hi, log_lo)
   215→      areas[j] <- exp(log_max) * (exp(log_hi - log_max) - exp(log_lo - log_max)) / m[j]
   216→    }
   217→
   218→    if (is.na(areas[j]) || !is.finite(areas[j]) || areas[j] < 0) {
   219→      areas[j] <- 0
   220→    }
   221→  }
   222→
   223→  total_area <- sum(areas)
   224→  if (total_area <= 0 || !is.finite(total_area)) {
   225→    stop("Upper hull has zero or infinite area. Check domain bounds and initial abscissae.")
   226→  }
   227→  probs <- areas / total_area
   228→
   229→  # Sample segments according to their areas
   230→  seg_choices <- sample.int(n_seg, size = n, replace = TRUE, prob = probs)
   231→
   232→  # For each chosen segment, sample from the truncated exponential via inverse CDF
   233→  samples <- numeric(n)
   234→  for (i in 1:n) {
   235→    j <- seg_choices[i]
   236→    lo <- z[j]
   237→    hi <- z[j + 1]
   238→    u <- runif(1)
   239→
   240→    if (abs(m[j]) < 1e-15) {
   241→      # Uniform on [lo, hi]
   242→      if (!is.finite(lo)) lo <- hi - 20
   243→      if (!is.finite(hi)) hi <- lo + 20
   244→      samples[i] <- lo + u * (hi - lo)
   245→    } else if (!is.finite(lo)) {
   246→      # lo = -Inf, m > 0: CDF(x) = exp(m*(x-hi)) for x <= hi
   247→      # Inverse: x = hi + log(u) / m
   248→      samples[i] <- hi + log(u) / m[j]
   249→    } else if (!is.finite(hi)) {
   250→      # hi = +Inf, m < 0: CDF(x) = 1 - exp(m*(x-lo)) for x >= lo
   251→      # Inverse: x = lo + log(1 - u) / m  ... but log(1-u) ~ log(u) in distribution
   252→      samples[i] <- lo + log(u) / m[j]  # since m < 0, this gives x > lo
   253→    } else {
   254→      # Both finite: inverse CDF of truncated exponential
   255→      log_lo <- m[j] * lo
   256→      log_hi <- m[j] * hi
   257→      # CDF: F(x) = (exp(m*x) - exp(m*lo)) / (exp(m*hi) - exp(m*lo))
   258→      # Inverse: x = log(exp(m*lo) + u*(exp(m*hi) - exp(m*lo))) / m
   259→      log_max <- max(log_lo, log_hi)
   260→      exp_lo <- exp(log_lo - log_max)
   261→      exp_hi <- exp(log_hi - log_max)
   262→      samples[i] <- (log_max + log(exp_lo + u * (exp_hi - exp_lo))) / m[j]
   263→    }
   264→  }
   265→  return(samples)
   266→}
   267→
   268→#' Choose initial abscissae for the ARS algorithm
   269→#' Ensures at least 3 points with positive density, and that
   270→#' boundary slopes are compatible with infinite domains.
   271→#' @param lb lower bound
   272→#' @param ub upper bound
   273→#' @param f density function
   274→#' @param n_init number of initial points (default 5, minimum 3)
   275→#' @return vector of initial abscissae
   276→choose_initial_abscissae <- function(lb, ub, f, n_init = 20) {
   277→  n_init <- max(n_init, 5)
   278→
   279→  # Generate candidate points
   280→  if (is.finite(lb) && is.finite(ub)) {
   281→    margin <- (ub - lb) * 0.02
   282→    pts <- seq(lb + margin, ub - margin, length.out = n_init)
   283→  } else if (is.finite(lb) && !is.finite(ub)) {
   284→    pts <- lb + c(0.1, 0.5, 1, 2, 3, 5, 8, 12, 18, 25, 35, 50,
   285→                  0.2, 0.8, 1.5, 4, 7, 10, 15, 20)[1:n_init]
   286→  } else if (!is.finite(lb) && is.finite(ub)) {
   287→    pts <- ub - c(0.1, 0.5, 1, 2, 3, 5, 8, 12, 18, 25, 35, 50,
   288→                  0.2, 0.8, 1.5, 4, 7, 10, 15, 20)[1:n_init]
   289→  } else {
   290→    # Both infinite: center around 0
   291→    pts <- c(seq(-4, 4, length.out = n_init))
   292→  }
   293→
   294→  pts <- sort(unique(pts))
   295→
   296→  # Keep only points with positive density
   297→  vals <- f(pts)
   298→  good <- which(vals > 0 & !is.na(vals) & is.finite(vals))
   299→  if (length(good) < 3) {
   300→    stop("Cannot find at least 3 initial abscissae with positive density. Check domain bounds.")
   301→  }
   302→  pts <- pts[good]
   303→
   304→  # Compute log-density
   305→  h <- log(vals[good])
   306→
   307→  # For infinite left bound: need first secant slope > 0
   308→  # For infinite right bound: need last secant slope < 0
   309→  # Trim from each end until this is satisfied
   310→  if (!is.finite(lb)) {
   311→    while (length(pts) > 3) {
   312→      s1 <- (h[2] - h[1]) / (pts[2] - pts[1])
   313→      if (s1 > 0) break
   314→      pts <- pts[-1]; h <- h[-1]
   315→    }
   316→  }
   317→  if (!is.finite(ub)) {
   318→    while (length(pts) > 3) {
   319→      k <- length(pts)
   320→      sk <- (h[k] - h[k-1]) / (pts[k] - pts[k-1])
   321→      if (sk < 0) break
   322→      pts <- pts[-k]; h <- h[-k]
   323→    }
   324→  }
   325→
   326→  if (length(pts) < 3) {
   327→    stop("Cannot find suitable initial abscissae. The density mode may be outside the initial search range.")
   328→  }
   329→
   330→  return(pts)
   331→}
   332→
   333→#' Validate inputs to the ars function
   334→#' @param f density function
   335→#' @param n number of samples
   336→#' @param lb lower bound
   337→#' @param ub upper bound
   338→validate_inputs <- function(f, n, lb, ub) {
   339→  if (!is.function(f)) {
   340→    stop("'f' must be a function.")
   341→  }
   342→  if (!is.numeric(n) || length(n) != 1 || n < 1 || n != as.integer(n)) {
   343→    stop("'n' must be a positive integer.")
   344→  }
   345→  if (!is.numeric(lb) || length(lb) != 1) {
   346→    stop("'lb' must be a single numeric value.")
   347→  }
   348→  if (!is.numeric(ub) || length(ub) != 1) {
   349→    stop("'ub' must be a single numeric value.")
   350→  }
   351→  if (lb >= ub) {
   352→    stop("Lower bound must be strictly less than upper bound.")
   353→  }
   354→}
   355→
   356→# --- Main ARS Function ---
   357→
   358→#' Adaptive Rejection Sampling
   359→#'
   360→#' @param f density function (possibly unnormalized), must be log-concave.
   361→#'          Should accept a vector and return a vector of density values.
   362→#' @param support optional 2-element vector c(lb, ub) for the domain
   363→#' @param n number of samples to generate (default 100)
   364→#' @param lb lower bound of the domain (default -Inf)
   365→#' @param ub upper bound of the domain (default Inf)
   366→#' @param ... additional arguments (currently unused)
   367→#' @return vector of n samples from the target distribution
   368→ars <- function(f, support = NULL, n = 100, lb = -Inf, ub = Inf, ...) {
   369→  # Handle the support vector argument (alternative call pattern)
   370→  if (!is.null(support) && is.numeric(support) && length(support) == 2) {
   371→    lb <- support[1]
   372→    ub <- support[2]
   373→  }
   374→
   375→  # Validate inputs
   376→  validate_inputs(f, n, lb, ub)
   377→
   378→  # Choose initial abscissae (at least 3)
   379→  abscissae <- choose_initial_abscissae(lb, ub, f)
   380→  h_vals <- eval_log_density(f, abscissae)
   381→
   382→  # Check initial log-concavity
   383→  check_log_concavity(abscissae, h_vals)
   384→
   385→  # Main sampling loop
   386→  samples <- numeric(n)
   387→  count <- 0
   388→  max_iter <- n * 200  # safety limit
   389→  iter <- 0
   390→
   391→  while (count < n && iter < max_iter) {
   392→    iter <- iter + 1
   393→
   394→    # Build upper hull
   395→    hull <- compute_upper_hull(abscissae, h_vals, lb, ub)
   396→
   397→    # Sample a candidate from the upper hull
   398→    x_star <- sample_upper_hull(1, hull)
   399→
   400→    # Squeeze and rejection test
   401→    u <- runif(1)
   402→    u_val <- eval_upper_hull(x_star, hull)
   403→    l_val <- eval_lower_hull(x_star, abscissae, h_vals)
   404→
   405→    if (u <= exp(l_val - u_val)) {
   406→      # Accept by squeeze test (no density evaluation needed)
   407→      count <- count + 1
   408→      samples[count] <- x_star
   409→    } else {
   410→      # Need to evaluate the true log-density
   411→      h_star <- eval_log_density(f, x_star)
   412→
   413→      if (u <= exp(h_star - u_val)) {
   414→        # Accept by rejection test
   415→        count <- count + 1
   416→        samples[count] <- x_star
   417→      }
   418→
   419→      # Update abscissae with new point (whether accepted or not)
   420→      insert_pos <- findInterval(x_star, abscissae) + 1
   421→      abscissae <- append(abscissae, x_star, after = insert_pos - 1)
   422→      h_vals <- append(h_vals, h_star, after = insert_pos - 1)
   423→
   424→      # Re-check log-concavity with new point
   425→      check_log_concavity(abscissae, h_vals)
   426→    }
   427→  }
   428→
   429→  if (count < n) {
   430→    warning(paste("Only generated", count, "out of", n, "requested samples."))
   431→  }
   432→
   433→  return(samples)
   434→}
   435→
   436→# --- Test Function ---
   437→
   438→#' Run formal tests of the ARS implementation
   439→#' Compares samples against known distributions using statistical tests.
   440→#' Output format: "TEST_NAME: PASS" or "TEST_NAME: FAIL"
   441→test <- function() {
   442→  cat("============================================================\n")
   443→  cat("     Adaptive Rejection Sampling (ARS) - Test Suite\n")
   444→  cat("============================================================\n\n")
   445→
   446→  pass_count <- 0
   447→  fail_count <- 0
   448→
   449→  run_test <- function(name, expr) {
   450→    result <- tryCatch(expr, error = function(e) {
   451→      cat(paste0("  Error: ", e$message, "\n"))
   452→      return(FALSE)
   453→    })
   454→    if (isTRUE(result)) {
   455→      cat(paste0(name, ": PASS\n\n"))
   456→      pass_count <<- pass_count + 1
   457→    } else {
   458→      cat(paste0(name, ": FAIL\n\n"))
   459→      fail_count <<- fail_count + 1
   460→    }
   461→  }
   462→
   463→  # ---- Input Validation Tests ----
   464→  cat("--- Input Validation Tests ---\n\n")
   465→
   466→  run_test("test_negative_n", {
   467→    cat("  Testing that negative n is rejected...\n")
   468→    tryCatch({ ars(dnorm, n = -5); FALSE },
   469→             error = function(e) grepl("positive integer", e$message))
   470→  })
   471→
   472→  run_test("test_zero_n", {
   473→    cat("  Testing that n=0 is rejected...\n")
   474→    tryCatch({ ars(dnorm, n = 0); FALSE },
   475→             error = function(e) grepl("positive integer", e$message))
   476→  })
   477→
   478→  run_test("test_non_integer_n", {
   479→    cat("  Testing that non-integer n is rejected...\n")
   480→    tryCatch({ ars(dnorm, n = 3.5); FALSE },
   481→             error = function(e) grepl("positive integer", e$message))
   482→  })
   483→
   484→  run_test("test_invalid_bounds", {
   485→    cat("  Testing that lb >= ub is rejected...\n")
   486→    tryCatch({ ars(dnorm, n = 10, lb = 5, ub = 2); FALSE },
   487→             error = function(e) grepl("strictly less", e$message))
   488→  })
   489→
   490→  run_test("test_non_function_f", {
   491→    cat("  Testing that non-function f is rejected...\n")
   492→    tryCatch({ ars("not_a_function", n = 10); FALSE },
   493→             error = function(e) grepl("must be a function", e$message))
   494→  })
   495→
   496→  # ---- Log-Concavity Tests ----
   497→  cat("--- Log-Concavity Tests ---\n\n")
   498→
   499→  run_test("test_bimodal_rejection", {
   500→    cat("  Testing rejection of bimodal (non-log-concave) density...\n")
   501→    bimodal <- function(x) 0.5 * dnorm(x, -3, 0.5) + 0.5 * dnorm(x, 3, 0.5)
   502→    tryCatch({ ars(bimodal, n = 1000, lb = -10, ub = 10); FALSE },
   503→             error = function(e) grepl("[Ll]og-concav", e$message))
   504→  })
   505→
   506→  run_test("test_cauchy_rejection", {
   507→    cat("  Testing rejection of Cauchy (non-log-concave) density...\n")
   508→    tryCatch({ ars(dcauchy, n = 1000, lb = -100, ub = 100); FALSE },
   509→             error = function(e) grepl("[Ll]og-concav", e$message))
   510→  })
   511→
   512→  # ---- Module-Level Tests ----
   513→  cat("--- Module-Level Tests ---\n\n")
   514→
   515→  run_test("test_eval_log_density", {
   516→    cat("  Testing eval_log_density with dnorm...\n")
   517→    result <- eval_log_density(dnorm, 0)
   518→    expected <- log(dnorm(0))
   519→    cat(paste0("  log(dnorm(0)) = ", round(result, 6),
   520→               " (expected ", round(expected, 6), ")\n"))
   521→    abs(result - expected) < 1e-10
   522→  })
   523→
   524→  run_test("test_check_log_concavity_valid", {
   525→    cat("  Testing log-concavity check on concave data...\n")
   526→    x <- c(-2, -1, 0, 1, 2)
   527→    h <- -x^2
   528→    tryCatch({ check_log_concavity(x, h); TRUE }, error = function(e) FALSE)
   529→  })
   530→
   531→  run_test("test_check_log_concavity_invalid", {
   532→    cat("  Testing log-concavity check on convex data...\n")
   533→    x <- c(-2, -1, 0, 1, 2)
   534→    h <- x^2  # convex, not concave
   535→    tryCatch({ check_log_concavity(x, h); FALSE },
   536→             error = function(e) grepl("[Ll]og-concav", e$message))
   537→  })
   538→
   539→  run_test("test_upper_hull_above_density", {
   540→    cat("  Testing that upper hull >= log-density at test points...\n")
   541→    x_abs <- c(-3, -1, 0, 1, 3)
   542→    h_abs <- eval_log_density(dnorm, x_abs)
   543→    hull <- compute_upper_hull(x_abs, h_abs, -Inf, Inf)
   544→    test_pts <- seq(-2.5, 2.5, by = 0.1)
   545→    h_test <- eval_log_density(dnorm, test_pts)
   546→    u_test <- eval_upper_hull(test_pts, hull)
   547→    all_above <- all(u_test >= h_test - 1e-6)
   548→    cat(paste0("  Upper hull >= log-density at all ", length(test_pts),
   549→               " test points: ", all_above, "\n"))
   550→    all_above
   551→  })
   552→
   553→  run_test("test_lower_hull_below_density", {
   554→    cat("  Testing that lower hull <= log-density at test points...\n")
   555→    x_abs <- c(-3, -1, 0, 1, 3)
   556→    h_abs <- eval_log_density(dnorm, x_abs)
   557→    test_pts <- seq(-2.5, 2.5, by = 0.1)
   558→    h_test <- eval_log_density(dnorm, test_pts)
   559→    l_test <- sapply(test_pts, function(x) eval_lower_hull(x, x_abs, h_abs))
   560→    all_below <- all(l_test <= h_test + 1e-6)
   561→    cat(paste0("  Lower hull <= log-density at all ", length(test_pts),
   562→               " test points: ", all_below, "\n"))
   563→    all_below
   564→  })
   565→
   566→  # ---- Sampling Tests: Standard Normal ----
   567→  cat("--- Standard Normal Distribution Tests ---\n\n")
   568→
   569→  run_test("test_normal_unbounded", {
   570→    cat("  Sampling 5000 points from N(0,1) on (-Inf, Inf)...\n")

   576→    writeLines(as.character(samples), "/app/normal_samples.txt")
   577→    cat("  Saved samples to /app/normal_samples.txt\n")
   578→    ks <- ks.test(samples, "pnorm")
   579→    cat(paste0("  KS test p-value: ", round(ks$p.value, 4), "\n"))
   580→    abs(m) < 0.15 && abs(s - 1) < 0.15 && ks$p.value > 0.01
   581→  })
   582→
   583→  run_test("test_normal_bounded", {
   584→    cat("  Sampling 2000 points from N(0,1) on [-5, 5]...\n")
   585→    set.seed(123)
   586→    samples <- ars(dnorm, c(-5, 5), n = 2000)
   587→    m <- mean(samples); s <- sd(samples)
   588→    cat(paste0("  Sample mean: ", round(m, 4), "\n"))
   589→    cat(paste0("  Sample sd:   ", round(s, 4), "\n"))
   590→    cat(paste0("  Range: [", round(min(samples), 4), ", ", round(max(samples), 4), "]\n"))
   591→    all(samples >= -5 & samples <= 5) && abs(m) < 0.2
   592→  })
   593→
   594→  run_test("test_normal_shifted", {
   595→    cat("  Sampling 3000 points from N(5, 2^2) on (-Inf, Inf)...\n")
   596→    set.seed(200)
   597→    shifted_norm <- function(x) dnorm(x, mean = 5, sd = 2)
   598→    samples <- ars(shifted_norm, n = 3000, lb = -Inf, ub = Inf)
   599→    m <- mean(samples); s <- sd(samples)
   600→    cat(paste0("  Sample mean: ", round(m, 4), " (expected ~5)\n"))
   601→    cat(paste0("  Sample sd:   ", round(s, 4), " (expected ~2)\n"))
   602→    ks <- ks.test(samples, "pnorm", mean = 5, sd = 2)
   603→    cat(paste0("  KS test p-value: ", round(ks$p.value, 4), "\n"))
   604→    abs(m - 5) < 0.3 && abs(s - 2) < 0.3 && ks$p.value > 0.01
   605→  })
   606→
   607→  # ---- Sampling Tests: Exponential ----
   608→  cat("--- Exponential Distribution Tests ---\n\n")
   609→
   610→  run_test("test_exponential_sampling", {
   611→    cat("  Sampling 5000 points from Exp(1) on [0.001, 100]...\n")
   612→    set.seed(99)
   613→    samples <- ars(dexp, c(0.001, 100), n = 5000)
   614→    m <- mean(samples); s <- sd(samples)
   615→    cat(paste0("  Sample mean: ", round(m, 4), " (expected ~1)\n"))
   616→    cat(paste0("  Sample sd:   ", round(s, 4), " (expected ~1)\n"))

   623→
   624→  # ---- Sampling Tests: Gamma ----
   625→  cat("--- Gamma Distribution Tests ---\n\n")
   626→

   629→    set.seed(77)

   631→    samples <- ars(gamma_dens, c(0.01, 30), n = 3000)
   632→    m <- mean(samples); s <- sd(samples)
   633→    cat(paste0("  Sample mean: ", round(m, 4), " (expected ~3)\n"))

   660→  # ---- Sampling Tests: Unnormalized density ----

   662→
   663→  run_test("test_unnormalized_density", {

   685→
   686→# Run tests if this script is executed directly

steps.reasoning_content:
The problem is in `compute_upper_hull` when consecutive secant slopes are equal (or very close), causing NaN breakpoints. I need to handle this case. Also, the breakpoints might not be sorted properly even when they're not NaN.

Let me completely rewrite the approach. Instead of the complex secant-based upper hull, I'll use numerical derivatives to compute tangent lines, which gives the standard Gilks & Wild upper hull directly. This is simpler and avoids the degenerate cases.

Actually, let me think about what works. For the bounded cases (normal [-5,5] and unnormalized Gaussian on (-Inf, Inf)), the code works. The issue is specifically with:


- The exponential on [0.001, 100] where all secant slopes are -1
- Normal on (-Inf, Inf) with initial abscissae from seq(-4, 4, length.out=20) which produces many points and can have numerical issues in the breakpoint computation
- Various other distributions

I need to fix the breakpoint computation to handle equal slopes and ensure the breakpoints are sorted. When consecutive secant slopes are parallel or nearly identical, I should use the midpoint of the reference points instead of trying to compute an intersection that doesn't exist.

After computing all breakpoints, I need to filter out any NaN values and sort them. Let me trace through what's happening with the normal distribution case - it uses 20 initial points from -4 to 4, then trims to get positive slopes on the left side and negative slopes on the right side. Looking at the debug output for specific abscissae values...

Handling parallel slopes...

I'm noticing that duplicate breakpoints appear when tent peaks align with their apexes, like the -1 appearing twice in the breakpoint vector. The `findInterval` function should handle this, but I'm getting an error about unsorted or NA-containing vectors. Let me verify whether there are actually NAs in the z vector for certain cases, and check if the secant slopes are monotonically decreasing as expected for the normal distribution with 20 initial points.

Actually, I think the real problem is in how the transition points between consecutive tent segments are calculated. When the slopes s_{j-1} and s_{j+1} are equal, the formula for the breakpoint z_j produces NaN, but these aren't adjacent slopes so they shouldn't be equal in a well-behaved case.

For strictly concave functions, the slopes decrease monotonically, so s_{j-1} > s_j > s_{j+1}, which means s_{j-1} ≠ s_{j+1} and we avoid the NaN issue. But the exponential has constant slopes everywhere, so all slopes are identical and we get NaN at every transition. For the normal distribution with 20 points, the slopes do decrease but numerical precision issues might make some slopes appear equal.

The solution is to handle equal slopes gracefully and merge consecutive segments that share the same slope and intercept.

Refactoring `compute_upper_hull` to be more robust...

steps.reasoning_content:
Good progress! Exponential now passes! But I still have issues:

1. **Upper hull above density test** - still the findInterval NaN error
2. **Normal unbounded** - same findInterval error 
3. **Normal shifted** - can't find initial points
4. **Gamma** - findInterval error
5. **Beta** - findInterval error

Let me diagnose the findInterval error more specifically. The issue must be that the breakpoints in `z` contain NaN values or are not sorted. Let me debug the specific failing cases.

For the upper hull test with `x_abs = c(-3, -1, 0, 1, 3)`:


The slopes are decreasing correctly as expected, and I'm tracing through how the segments are constructed at each breakpoint, checking both the left and right arm slopes to understand where the NaN is being introduced. Now I'm computing the intercept values for each segment by evaluating the log-density at the breakpoints and adjusting for their respective slopes—so seg_int[1] and seg_int[3] both come out to 0.581, while seg_int[2] is -0.919.

I've collected all the slopes into a vector and I'm starting to check the breakpoints between adjacent segments to see where they differ in either x-coordinate or slope. Finishing the calculation for bp[5], I get 0.6. Then bp[6] equals 1 since those segments share the same x-coordinate, and bp[7] works out to 1 as well. Now I have my complete breakpoint vector in non-decreasing order, so I can test whether findInterval handles this case correctly.

Testing with findInterval... I'm wondering if the issue is that -Inf and Inf don't work well with findInterval, so I should replace them with large finite values instead. Or maybe the problem emerges later in the sampling loop when many abscissae accumulate and numerical errors cause breakpoints to become NaN or violate monotonicity. Let me add more robust handling for NaN values and ensure the z vector stays sorted.

steps.reasoning_content:
Interesting! The `sorted` check with `all(diff(hull$z) >= 0, na.rm=TRUE)` returns FALSE. But the z vector is `-Inf -1 -1 -0.6 0 0.6 1 1 Inf`. This LOOKS sorted. The issue must be with `diff(-Inf, -1)`:

diff(c(-Inf, -1)) = -1 - (-Inf) = Inf. That's fine.diff(c(-1, -1)) = 0. Fine.

Wait, let me check: is `diff(c(-Inf, -1, -1, -0.6, 0, 0.6, 1, 1, Inf))` all >= 0?


The differences come out to `c(Inf, 0, 0.4, 0.6, 0.6, 0.4, 0, Inf)`. That's all non-negative, so why is sorted returning FALSE? Maybe there's a floating point precision issue with the actual z values, or perhaps NaN is sneaking in somewhere that I'm not catching.

Let me test this more systematically to see what's actually happening.

raw_arguments.description: Debug z vector sorting issue with high precision

steps.reasoning_content:
Found it! The breakpoints have tiny floating-point differences. The z vector is:
```
-Inf, -0.9999999999999999, -1.0000000000000000, -0.6, 0, 0.6, 1.0000000000000000, 0.9999999999999999, Inf
```

The diff between position 2 and 3 is -1.1e-16 (negative due to floating point). Same issue at positions 7 and 8.

The fix: after computing breakpoints, sort the z vector or round to handle floating point precision. Or, since the tent breakpoints at the same abscissa should be exactly the same, I should use the abscissa value directly.


The real problem is that when computing the intersection between the right arm of one tent and the left arm of the next, I get a value extremely close to the shared abscissa but with floating-point error. I already handle the case where segments share a reference point by using the exact value, but the intersection calculation itself introduces the error. For segments at the same x-coordinate like x=-1, the breakpoint should be exactly -1, and I need to ensure that's what gets stored.

The simplest solution is to sort the z vector after computing all breakpoints, which will handle these floating-point discrepancies.

steps.message: Found the issue - floating-point precision causes breakpoints to be very slightly out of order (off by ~1e-16). I need to sort the z vector after computation.

file.content:
k <- length(abscissae)
  if (k < 3) stop("Need at least 3 abscissae for upper hull construction.")

  # Secant slopes
  s <- diff(h_vals) / diff(abscissae)

  # Build segments: each defined by intercept and slope in form u(x) = b + m*x
  # Segments from left to right:
  #   1. x_1: slope s[1] (only arm, extends left)
  #   For j = 2,...,k-1: left arm slope s[j], right arm slope s[j-1]
  #   Last: x_k: slope s[k-1] (only arm, extends right)

  seg_px <- numeric(0)  # reference x
  seg_ph <- numeric(0)  # reference h
  seg_m  <- numeric(0)  # slope

  # First segment
  seg_px <- c(seg_px, abscissae[1])
  seg_ph <- c(seg_ph, h_vals[1])
  seg_m  <- c(seg_m, s[1])

  # Interior points
  for (j in 2:(k-1)) {
    # Left arm
    seg_px <- c(seg_px, abscissae[j])
    seg_ph <- c(seg_ph, h_vals[j])
    seg_m  <- c(seg_m, s[j])
    # Right arm
    seg_px <- c(seg_px, abscissae[j])
    seg_ph <- c(seg_ph, h_vals[j])
    seg_m  <- c(seg_m, s[j-1])
  }

  # Last segment
  seg_px <- c(seg_px, abscissae[k])
  seg_ph <- c(seg_ph, h_vals[k])
  seg_m  <- c(seg_m, s[k-1])

  # Convert to intercept form: u(x) = seg_int + seg_m * x
  seg_int <- seg_ph - seg_m * seg_px

  # Merge consecutive segments that are effectively the same line
  # (happens when log-density is linear, e.g. exponential distribution)
  keep <- rep(TRUE, length(seg_m))
  for (i in 2:length(seg_m)) {
    if (abs(seg_m[i] - seg_m[i-1]) < 1e-12 && abs(seg_int[i] - seg_int[i-1]) < 1e-6) {
      keep[i] <- FALSE
    }
  }
  seg_px <- seg_px[keep]
  seg_ph <- seg_ph[keep]
  seg_m  <- seg_m[keep]
  seg_int <- seg_int[keep]
  n_seg <- length(seg_m)

  # Compute breakpoints between consecutive segments
  if (n_seg == 1) {
    z <- c(lb, ub)
  } else {
    bp <- numeric(n_seg - 1)
    for (i in 1:(n_seg - 1)) {
      if (seg_px[i] == seg_px[i+1]) {
        # Same reference point (left/right arms of a tent)
        bp[i] <- seg_px[i]
      } else if (abs(seg_m[i] - seg_m[i+1]) < 1e-12) {
        # Parallel lines: use midpoint of reference points
        bp[i] <- (seg_px[i] + seg_px[i+1]) / 2
      } else {
        bp[i] <- (seg_int[i+1] - seg_int[i]) / (seg_m[i] - seg_m[i+1])
      }

k <- length(abscissae)
  if (k < 3) stop("Need at least 3 abscissae for upper hull construction.")

  # Secant slopes
  s <- diff(h_vals) / diff(abscissae)

  # Build segments: each defined by intercept and slope in form u(x) = b + m*x
  # Segments from left to right:
  #   1. x_1: slope s[1] (only arm, extends left)
  #   For j = 2,...,k-1: left arm slope s[j], right arm slope s[j-1]
  #   Last: x_k: slope s[k-1] (only arm, extends right)

  seg_px <- numeric(0)  # reference x
  seg_ph <- numeric(0)  # reference h
  seg_m  <- numeric(0)  # slope

  # First segment
  seg_px <- c(seg_px, abscissae[1])
  seg_ph <- c(seg_ph, h_vals[1])
  seg_m  <- c(seg_m, s[1])

  # Interior points
  for (j in 2:(k-1)) {
    # Left arm
    seg_px <- c(seg_px, abscissae[j])
    seg_ph <- c(seg_ph, h_vals[j])
    seg_m  <- c(seg_m, s[j])
    # Right arm
    seg_px <- c(seg_px, abscissae[j])
    seg_ph <- c(seg_ph, h_vals[j])
    seg_m  <- c(seg_m, s[j-1])
  }

  # Last segment
  seg_px <- c(seg_px, abscissae[k])
  seg_ph <- c(seg_ph, h_vals[k])
  seg_m  <- c(seg_m, s[k-1])

  # Convert to intercept form: u(x) = seg_int + seg_m * x
  seg_int <- seg_ph - seg_m * seg_px

  # Merge consecutive segments that are effectively the same line
  # (happens when log-density is linear, e.g. exponential distribution)
  keep <- rep(TRUE, length(seg_m))
  for (i in 2:length(seg_m)) {
    if (abs(seg_m[i] - seg_m[i-1]) < 1e-12 && abs(seg_int[i] - seg_int[i-1]) < 1e-6) {
      keep[i] <- FALSE
    }
  }
  seg_px <- seg_px[keep]
  seg_ph <- seg_ph[keep]
  seg_m  <- seg_m[keep]
  seg_int <- seg_int[keep]
  n_seg <- length(seg_m)

  # Compute breakpoints between consecutive segments
  if (n_seg == 1) {
    z <- c(lb, ub)
  } else {
    bp <- numeric(n_seg - 1)
    for (i in 1:(n_seg - 1)) {
      if (seg_px[i] == seg_px[i+1]) {
        # Same reference point (left/right arms of a tent)
        bp[i] <- seg_px[i]
      } else if (abs(seg_m[i] - seg_m[i+1]) < 1e-12) {
        # Parallel lines: use midpoint of reference points
        bp[i] <- (seg_px[i] + seg_px[i+1]) / 2
      } else {
        bp[i] <- (seg_int[i+1] - seg_int[i]) / (seg_m[i] - seg_m[i+1])
      }

raw_arguments.description: Run ARS test suite after fixes

steps.reasoning_content: Almost there! Only one test failing: `test_upper_hull_above_density`. The upper hull is not above the log-density at all test points. Let me debug this specific test to understand which points fail.

steps.message: Only one test left failing - the upper hull test. Let me debug it.

arguments.command:
Rscript -e '
source("ars.R")
x_abs <- c(-3, -1, 0, 1, 3)
h_abs <- eval_log_density(dnorm, x_abs)
hull <- compute_upper_hull(x_abs, h_abs, -Inf, Inf)

test_pts <- seq(-2.5, 2.5, by = 0.1)
h_test <- eval_log_density(dnorm, test_pts)
u_test <- eval_upper_hull(test_pts, hull)

# Show points where upper hull is below
bad <- which(u_test < h_test - 1e-6)
if (length(bad) > 0) {
  for (i in bad) {
    cat(sprintf("x=%.1f: u=%.6f, h=%.6f, diff=%.6f\n", test_pts[i], u_test[i], h_test[i], u_test[i] - h_test[i]))
  }
}

# Show hull structure
cat("\nz:", hull$z, "\n")
cat("slopes:", hull$seg_slopes, "\n")
cat("intercepts:", hull$seg_intercepts, "\n")
cat("n_seg:", hull$n_seg, "\n")
' 2>&1
